{
"id": 275,
"attributes": {
"name": "Security clearance related services - Submission of Report by Special Branch (SB) for Visa",
"description": "All foreign nationals/expatriates employed in companies under the jurisdiction of the Bangladesh Economic Zones Authority (BEZA) must obtain Security Clearance from the Ministry of Home Affairs (MoHA), after securing a valid Work Permit.\n Security Clearance is a mandatory requirement for visa extension, immigration compliance, and lawful continuation of employment in Bangladesh. Applications must be submitted through the BEZA One Stop Service (OSS) Portal within twenty-one (21) days of obtaining the Work Permit. Failure to do so may result in complications regarding visa extension and other immigration matters. Otherwise, it may arise complications regarding visa extension and any other immigration related issues in future.",
"shortDescription": "Security clearance related services - Submission of Report by Special Branch (SB) for Visa",
"applicationUrl": "https://oss.beza.gov.bd/",
"isActive": true,
"createdAt": "2025-08-04T10:21:07.397Z",
"updatedAt": "2026-01-17T21:29:22.620Z",
"publishedAt": "2025-08-04T10:21:08.079Z",
"order": 27,
"locale": "en",
"synonyms": [
"SB Report for Visa",
"Special Branch Clearance",
"Police Verification for Visa",
"Security Report Submission"
],
"ValidityTime": "Depending on the Visa Criteria and Work permit tenure",
"applicationButtonText": "Apply",
"validityText": "Depending on the Visa Criteria and Work permit tenure",
"requiredDocumentText": "Required Document",
"isRedirection": null,
"applicationFeeText": "BDT 500 + 15% VAT  - Applicable bank charges + 15% VAT on bank charges",
"slaText": "Preparation of Security Report:  × 30 days – by NSI  × 45 days – by SB  Issuance of Security Clearance (NOC):  × 3 working days – MoHA, upon receipt of both reports.",
"saveforLaterButtonText": "পরবর্তীতে সংরক্ষণ করুন",
"isProvidedByBanglaBiz": null,
"shortName": null,
"serviceType": null,
"fee": "BDT 500 + 15% VAT  - Applicable bank charges + 15% VAT on bank charges",
"redirectApplicationUrl": null,
"requiredDocuments": [],
"processingTime": {
"id": 3744,
"minTime": null,
"maxTime": null,
"type": "Days"
},
"metadata": null,
"actAndRules": {
"id": 104,
"title": "Act And Rules",
"tabMenuName": "Act And Rules",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
},
"actAndRulesList": [
{
"id": 103,
"order": 1,
"actAndRulesType": "Act",
"actAndRulesDetails": "1. Bangladesh Economic Zones Act, 2010 (Act No. 42 of 2010)\n 2. Sub-Rule (2)(a) of Rule 3 of the “One Stop Service (Bangladesh Economic Zones Authority) Rules, 2018” read with Section 11 of the “One Stop Service Act, 2018”",
"actAndRulesFile": {
"data": null
}
}
]
},
"serviceDetailsMenuList": [
{
"id": 253,
"order": 1,
"tabMenuName": "About",
"description": "1. The employer/company, on behalf of the expatriate, submits the Security Clearance application to Ministry of Home Affairs through the BEZA-OSS Portal, along with all required documents.\n 2. The MoHA reviews the submitted information and documents, and assigns the application to the National Security Intelligence (NSI) and the Special Branch (SB) for preparation of the Security Report.\n 3. Upon receipt of the Security Reports from NSI and SB, MoHA processes and issues the Security Clearance Certificate (NOC) in favor of the expatriate.\n 4. The applicant (employer/company) obtains the Security Clearance Certificate both:\n × Via registered email; and\n × By downloading it directly from the BEZA-OSS Portal",
"title": "About the Approval",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
},
{
"id": 254,
"order": 2,
"tabMenuName": "Who can Apply",
"description": "Foreign nationals intending to invest, work, or participate in activities within Economic Zones (EZs) under the jurisdiction of the Bangladesh Economic Zones Authority (BEZA) must obtain the appropriate visa through the nearest Bangladeshi Embassy/High Commission/Consulate.",
"title": "Who can apply",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
}
],

{
"id": 261,
"attributes": {
"name": "Repatriation of Technical know-how and Assistance fee - In case of exceeding 6% of the cost of imported machinery",
"description": "Unit investors within BEZA-managed Economic Zones often require specialized technical know-how and technical assistance from foreign firms or experts to establish, operate, or enhance their production facilities. Payments made for such services, particularly when linked to imported capital machinery, are subject to regulatory limits and scrutiny to ensure alignment with Bangladesh’s foreign exchange and investment policies.",
"shortDescription": "Unit investors within BEZA-managed Economic Zones often require specialized technical know-how and technical assistance",
"applicationUrl": "https://oss.beza.gov.bd/",
"isActive": true,
"createdAt": "2025-08-04T10:13:07.706Z",
"updatedAt": "2026-01-17T14:26:21.613Z",
"publishedAt": "2025-08-04T10:13:08.507Z",
"order": 20,
"locale": "en",
"synonyms": [
"Technical Fee Remittance",
"Know-how Fee Transfer",
"Technical Assistance Fee",
"Tech Fee Transfer"
],
"ValidityTime": "× The Letter of Approval issued by BEZA shall remain valid for a period of forty-five (45) days from the date of issuance.\n  × In cases where the amount of remittance does not exceed six percent (6%) of the cost of imported machinery, the Applicant shall not be required to obtain prior approval from BEZA.",
"applicationButtonText": "Apply",
"validityText": "× The Letter of Approval issued by BEZA shall remain valid for a period of forty-five (45) days from the date of issuance.\n  × In cases where the amount of remittance does not exceed six percent (6%) of the cost of imported machinery, the Applicant shall not be required to obtain prior approval from BEZA.",
"requiredDocumentText": "Required Documents",
"isRedirection": null,
"applicationFeeText": "Application Fee: BDT 500 (Five Hundred Taka only)15% VAT on the fee   × Applicable bank charges for processing the payment   × 15% VAT on those bank charges",
"slaText": "The entire process shall be completed within seven (07) working days, provided that the application is complete in all respects and accompanied by the requisite documents.",
"saveforLaterButtonText": "Save for Later",
"isProvidedByBanglaBiz": null,
"shortName": null,
"serviceType": null,
"fee": "Application Fee: BDT 500 (Five Hundred Taka only)15% VAT on the fee   × Applicable bank charges for processing the payment   × 15% VAT on those bank charges",
"redirectApplicationUrl": null,
"requiredDocuments": [],
"processingTime": {
"id": 3388,
"minTime": "5",
"maxTime": "7",
"type": "Days"
},
"metadata": null,
"actAndRules": {
"id": 33,
"title": "Acts and Rules",
"tabMenuName": "Acts and Rules",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
},
"actAndRulesList": [
{
"id": 33,
"order": 1,
"actAndRulesType": "Act",
"actAndRulesDetails": "1. Bangladesh Economic Zones Act, 2010 (Act No. 42 of 2010)\n  2. Sub-Rule (2)(a) of Rule 3 of the “One Stop Service (Bangladesh Economic Zones Authority) Rules, 2018” read with Section 11 of the “One Stop Service Act, 2018”\n  3. GFET Guideline 2018",
"actAndRulesFile": {
"data": null
}
}
]
},
"serviceDetailsMenuList": [
{
"id": 119,
"order": 2,
"tabMenuName": "Who can Apply",
"description": "• Unit investors/Industrial Units situated in BEZA Economic Zones seeking to remit technical know-how or technical assistance fees to foreign experts, consultants, or firms.\n  • Cases where remittances exceed 6% of the cost of imported capital machinery as declared in import documentation or income tax filings.\n  • Authorized Dealer (AD) Banks, which effect the remittance upon receipt of BEZA’s approval and supporting documents.",
"title": "Who can Apply",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
},
{
"id": 120,
"order": 1,
"tabMenuName": "About",
"description": "Step 1: Submission of Application\n  The Applicant shall submit the Application Form (RTKHA-01), along with all required documents, online through the BEZA-OSS Portal.\n  \n  Step 2: Scrutiny by BEZA\n  BEZA-OSSC shall review and verify the submitted application and attached documents.\n  \n  Step 3: Evaluation and Approval\n  If the application and documents are found to be in order, the concerned officer shall issue a Letter of Approval in favor of the Applicant through the BEZA-OSS Portal.",
"title": "About the Approval",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
}
],
"serviceRequiredDocument": {
"id": 36,
"title": "Service Required",
"tabMenuName": "Documents",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
},
"requiredDocumnetLIst": [
{
"id": 621,
"order": 1,
"name": "Duly filled-in Application Form (RTKHA-01) as available on the BEZA-OSSC website.",
"description": null
},
{
"id": 624,
"order": 2,
"name": "Copy of Agreement/Contract between the Applicant and the Foreign Expert/Firm for provision of technical know-how and/or technical assistance.",
"description": null
},
{
"id": 623,
"order": 3,
"name": "Statement of the price/value of imported capital machinery, as stated in the Letter of Credit, duly attested by the relevant bank and the Applicant; or as declared in the previous year’s Income Tax Return",
"description": null
},
{
"id": 622,
"order": 4,
"name": "Copy of visa and work permit issued by BEZA for the foreign expert (if applicable)",
"description": null
},
{
"id": 625,
"order": 5,
"name": "Evidence of receipt of services relating to technical know-how/assistance, issued by the Applicant, such as a copy of the invoice of the Foreign Expert/Firm, duly approved, signed, and sealed by the Applicant",
"description": null
}
]
}
}
},
{
"id": 471,
"attributes": {
"name": "NOC for creation of 2nd charge on assets and properties",
"description": "NOC for creation of 2nd charge on assets and properties is an official approval required to create a secondary charge on existing assets or properties in favor of a financial institution. This permission allows organizations to obtain additional financing while maintaining compliance with regulatory and institutional requirements. Applicants must submit a formal application along with the sanction letter issued by the concerned commercial bank and relevant No Objection Certificates (NOCs) from existing banks, where applicable. In cases of pari-passu charge creation, NOCs from all concerned banks are mandatory. A dues clearance certificate may also be required. Upon approval, the second charge can be lawfully created within the stipulated timeline without any applicable fees.",
"shortDescription": "NOC for creation of 2nd charge on assets and properties\n",
"applicationUrl": "https://www.bepza.gov.bd/",
"isActive": true,
"createdAt": "2025-08-28T10:52:57.294Z",
"updatedAt": "2026-01-25T10:18:05.491Z",
"publishedAt": "2025-08-28T10:52:58.377Z",
"order": 29,
"locale": "en",
"synonyms": [
"Second Mortgage NOC",
"Collateral Creation Clearance",
"Security Interest NOC",
"Asset Charge Permission"
],

},
"serviceDetailsMenuList": [
{
"id": 304,
"order": 1,
"tabMenuName": "About ",
"description": "NOC for creation of 2nd charge on assets and properties is a mandatory administrative approval designed to regulate the creation of additional financial charges on assets already encumbered by a primary lender. This approval ensures transparency, financial discipline, and protection of institutional interests.\nTo obtain this NOC, the applicant must submit a formal application along with a sanction letter from the concerned commercial bank clearly indicating the proposed financing arrangement. In cases where the second charge is created on a pari-passu basis, NOCs from all concerned banks must be provided. If the assets are already charged with another bank, an NOC from the previous bank is also required. Where applicable, a dues clearance certificate must be submitted to confirm compliance with outstanding obligations.\nThe competent authority reviews all documents to ensure regulatory compliance and financial viability. The standard processing time for this service is 07 days, and no fee is applicable. Upon approval, the second charge may be created in accordance with the approved terms and conditions.",
"title": "About the Approval",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
},
{
"id": 785,
"order": 2,
"tabMenuName": "Who can Apply ",
"description": null,

"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
}

{
"id": 308,
"attributes": {
"name": "Permission for Bond-to-Bond Transfer, Machine Loan, and Repairing Machine",
"description": "This service provides permission for Bond-to-Bond transfer of goods, machine loans, and repairing of machines within Export Processing Zones (EPZs) under BEPZA supervision. Applicants must submit an application, invoice, sales contract, related import documents with bill of entry, and prior permission from the BEPZA Executive Office (as applicable). The fully digital process through the One Stop Service platform ensures approval within 02 working days.",
"shortDescription": "Permission for Bond-to-Bond Transfer, Machine Loan, and Repairing Machine",

"description": "The Permission for Bond-to-Bond Transfer, Machine Loan, and Repairing Machine service, facilitated through the One Stop Service platform, allows enterprises in Export Processing Zones to efficiently manage bonded goods and machinery under customs regulations. This permission is vital for inter-zone transfers, temporary loans of equipment, or repairs without breaking bond conditions, ensuring compliance while minimizing operational disruptions.\nThe process aligns with relevant customs and EPZ regulations, coordinated by authorities such as BEPZA. Required documents include a formal application, commercial invoice, sales contract, import-related documents along with the bill of entry, and any prior permission from the BEPZA Executive Office where applicable.\nApplications are submitted digitally via the OSS platform for swift verification of authenticity and compliance. Upon review, approval is granted within 02 working days, enabling enterprises to proceed with transfers, loans, or repairs seamlessly. Physical consultation is encouraged for complex cases or document preparation guidance.\nThis efficient service supports smooth supply chain management, reduces downtime for machinery maintenance, and reinforces the bonded warehouse benefits that attract export-oriented investments, contributing to enhanced productivity and competitiveness in Bangladesh's EPZs.",

{
"id": 377,
"attributes": {
"name": "Recommendation for Temporary Bond License",
"description": "The Recommendation for Temporary Bond License is a service provided by BEPZA to facilitate investors in establishing bonded warehouse facilities within Export Processing Zones (EPZs). This service allows investors to temporarily store imported raw materials and machinery before formal customs clearance. Investors are required to submit an application along with supporting documents, including the sanction letter, lease agreement, possession certificate, and company registration documents. BEPZA reviews the submission to ensure compliance with regulatory requirements. Upon approval, a recommendation for issuing a temporary bond license is granted. The process is designed to be efficient, typically completed within 3 days, enabling investors to manage imports smoothly and commence production activities without delays.",
"shortDescription": "Recommendation for Temporary Bond License",
"applicationUrl": "https://www.bepza.gov.bd/",
"isActive": true,
"createdAt": "2025-08-28T09:27:55.142Z",
"updatedAt": "2026-01-25T09:41:46.064Z",
"publishedAt": "2025-08-28T09:27:56.308Z",
"order": 4,
"locale": "en",
"synonyms": [
"Temporary Bonded Warehouse Recommendation",
"Interim Bond License Support",
"Provisional Bond Clearance"
],
"ValidityTime": null,
"applicationButtonText": "Apply",
"validityText": null,

"serviceDetailsMenuList": [
{
"id": 280,
"order": 1,
"tabMenuName": "About ",
"description": "The Recommendation for Temporary Bond License is a formal approval provided by BEPZA to allow investors to operate a bonded warehouse within an EPZ for temporarily storing imported raw materials, machinery, and other inputs. To obtain this recommendation, investors must submit an application along with relevant supporting documents, including a copy of the project sanction letter, lease agreement for the industrial plot or building, possession handing over certificate, and company registration documents such as the Memorandum & Articles of Association and Certificate of Incorporation. BEPZA verifies the submissions to ensure compliance with EPZ regulations and procedural requirements. Once approved, the recommendation enables the investor to apply for the Temporary Bond License from the customs authority, facilitating the import, storage, and management of project materials. This service ensures timely commencement of industrial operations while maintaining regulatory oversight. The typical processing time for this service is 3 working days, promoting efficiency and investor convenience.",
"title": "About the Approval",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
},

{
"id": 410,
"attributes": {
"name": "Issuance of Subcontract Permission (Offering/ Taking)",
"description": "Issuance of Subcontract Permission is a service provided by BEPZA to allow EPZ-based companies to legally offer or take subcontracts for production activities. Investors or companies must submit an application along with a non-judicial stamped subcontract agreement, a detailed list of raw materials and consumption, updated bond licenses of the subcontractor, supporting export orders or L/Cs, and reports of ongoing subcontracts. BEPZA reviews these documents to ensure compliance with regulatory guidelines, proper resource allocation, and lawful subcontracting practices. Upon approval, the subcontract permission is issued, enabling companies to manage production efficiently while maintaining transparency and adherence to legal and operational standards within Export Processing Zones.",
"shortDescription": "Issuance of Subcontract Permission (Offering/ Taking)\n",
"applicationUrl": "https://www.bepza.gov.bd/",
"isActive": true,
"createdAt": "2025-08-28T10:12:29.124Z",
"updatedAt": "2026-01-25T09:58:23.800Z",
"publishedAt": "2025-08-28T10:12:47.146Z",
"order": 15,
"locale": "en",

"Subcontract Permission Issuance",
"Approval for Subcontracting (Offer / Receive)",
"Subcontract Authorization",
"Subcontracting Approval"
],

"serviceDetailsMenuList": [
{
"id": 285,
"order": 1,
"tabMenuName": "About ",
"description": "The Issuance of Subcontract Permission (Offering/ Taking) is a formal approval process administered by BEPZA that allows companies within EPZs to engage in subcontracting arrangements for production. To obtain this approval, companies must submit a detailed application requesting permission to offer or take a subcontract, a subcontract agreement stamped with a non-judicial stamp of Tk. 300, a comprehensive list of raw materials and accessories along with their consumption, updated bond licenses of subcontractors, supporting export orders or letters of credit, and a report on ongoing subcontract work. BEPZA reviews all submissions to ensure compliance with legal and operational regulations, verifies the legitimacy of the subcontractor, and confirms that production and export processes are properly documented. Upon successful review, permission is granted, enabling the company to proceed with subcontracted production activities. This process promotes operational efficiency, regulatory compliance, and transparency while safeguarding the quality and legality of EPZ operations. The typical processing timeline for this service is three working days.",
"title": "About the Approval",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
},

{
"id": 424,
"attributes": {
"name": "Permission to take out Wastage, including Packing, Refuge, Iron Scrap Materials & others",
"description": "Permission to Take Out Wastage is a service provided by BEPZA that allows companies within EPZs to legally dispose of or sell their industrial wastage, including packing materials, refuge, iron scrap, and other residues. Investors must submit an application along with a detailed list of the materials specifying quantity, a copy of the sales agreement or agreement with the buyer, and a copy of the buyer’s valid license. BEPZA reviews the submission to ensure regulatory compliance, proper documentation, and lawful disposal practices. Upon approval, companies are granted permission to transport and sell their wastage materials, promoting efficient waste management, environmental safety, and operational transparency within the Export Processing Zones.",
"shortDescription": "Permission to take out Wastage, including Packing, Refuge, Iron Scrap Materials & others\n",
"applicationUrl": "https://www.bepza.gov.bd/",
"isActive": true,
"createdAt": "2025-08-28T10:24:05.047Z",

"serviceDetailsMenuList": [
{
"id": 287,
"order": 1,
"tabMenuName": "About ",
"description": "The Permission to Take Out Wastage, including packing, refuge, iron scrap materials, and other residues, is a formal approval process administered by BEPZA for companies operating within EPZs. To obtain this permission, companies must submit an application along with a detailed list of wastage materials, specifying quantities in tons, kilograms, or trucks. Additionally, applicants must provide a copy of the sales agreement or agreement with the buyer and a valid license of the party receiving the materials. BEPZA reviews the submitted documents to ensure that the disposal or sale of wastage complies with all regulatory requirements, environmental safety standards, and operational guidelines. Once the review is complete, the authority grants formal permission, enabling companies to transport and dispose of or sell their wastage materials legally. This service ensures proper waste management, maintains transparency, and supports sustainable operational practices. The typical processing timeline for this service is three working days.",
"title": "About the Approval",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
},

{
"id": 1022,
"attributes": {
"name": "Partnership Firm Registration ",
"description": "Partnership Firm Registration is a registration granted by the Directorate of Joint Stock Companies and Firms (RJSC) to legally operate a partnership business in Bangladesh. The application can be made through a completely online process (app.roc.gov.bd). The registration fee is 1400 taka, and the registration certificate is usually issued within 30 days.",
"shortDescription": "Partnership Firm Registration is a registration granted by the Directorate of Joint Stock Companies and Firms (RJSC)",
"applicationUrl": "https://roc.gov.bd/",
"isActive": true,
"createdAt": "2025-12-09T06:35:53.563Z",
"updatedAt": "2026-01-20T05:10:43.286Z",
"publishedAt": "2025-12-09T06:36:23.908Z",
"order": 3,
"locale": "en",

"Partnership Business Registration",
"Partnership Entity Registration",
"Legal Registration of Partnership"
],

"applicationButtonText": "Apply",

"isProvidedByBanglaBiz": false,

"serviceDetailsMenuList": [
{
"id": 789,
"order": 1,
"tabMenuName": "About",
"description": "Partnership Firm Registration is an optional but recommended legal process for two or more persons to run a joint business in Bangladesh as per the Partnership Act, 1932. If not registered, the business name is not protected, and there may be complications in obtaining legal benefits (such as a bank account or trade license). Registration is provided by the Directorate of Joint Stock Companies and Firms (RJSC).The application has to be made completely online. The main documents include detailed information of the partners (voter ID number 10 or 17 digits, passport number and scan copy of the first page if there is no NID, TIN number, mobile number, etc.), a scan copy of the partnership agreement, and a scan copy of the signature page with the names of two witnesses, NID number, mobile number, and date of birth.The registration fee of 1400 taka can be deposited in the bank through an automatically determined money receipt online or paid with a debit/credit/Visa card. After verification of the documents, the registration certificate is usually issued within 30 days. Through this registration, the firm receives legal recognition, and business operations become easier.",
"title": "About The Approval",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
},
{
"id": 790,
"order": 2,
"tabMenuName": "Who can Apply",
"description": "তথ্য শীঘ্রই যুক্ত করা হবে",
"title": "Who can Apply",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
}
],

"tabMenuName": "Documents",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
},
"requiredDocumnetLIst": [
{
"id": 3510,
"order": 1,
"name": "Voter ID number (10 or 17 digits) must be provided in the online registration form.",
"description": null
},
{
"id": 3511,
"order": 2,
"name": "If a National ID is not available, the Bangladesh passport number must be provided along with a scanned copy of the first page of the passport.",
"description": null
},
{
"id": 3512,
"order": 3,
"name": "Other details, including TIN number and mobile number, must also be completed.",
"description": null
},
{
"id": 3513,
"order": 4,
"name": "A scanned copy of the Partnership Agreement must be uploaded online.",
"description": null
},
{
"id": 3514,
"order": 5,
"name": "The names, NID numbers, mobile numbers, dates of birth, and signed pages of two witnesses must also be scanned and submitted online.",
"description": null
}
]
}
}

{
"id": 1338,
"attributes": {
"name": "NGO Registration Certificate",
"description": "NGO Registration Certificate is a mandatory registration issued by the NGO Affairs Bureau (NGOAB) for non-governmental organizations operating in Bangladesh that receive foreign donations to conduct voluntary activities. This registration is granted in accordance with the Foreign Donations (Voluntary Activities) Regulation Act, 2016.\n\nThrough this registration, NGOs are legally authorized to receive foreign funds, implement projects, and operate under government supervision. The registration process includes security clearance from the Ministry of Home Affairs, verification of required documents, and payment of prescribed fees. Receiving foreign donations without registration is illegal.\n\nThis registration ensures transparency, accountability, and effective state oversight of NGO activities. It is mandatory for local NGOs as well if they receive foreign funding. The registration is valid for a period of ten (10) years and must be renewed upon expiry.",
"shortDescription": "NGO Registration Certificate is a mandatory registration issued by the NGO Affairs Bureau (NGOAB) ",
"applicationUrl": "https://ngoab.gov.bd/",
"isActive": true,
"createdAt": "2026-01-23T10:33:18.219Z",
"updatedAt": "2026-01-23T10:52:03.012Z",
"publishedAt": "2026-01-23T10:34:45.991Z",
"order": 1,
"locale": "en",
"synonyms": [
"NGO Registration Certificate",
"Non-Governmental Organization Registration",
"NGO License",
"Registered NGO Certificate"
],
"ValidityTime": null,
"applicationButtonText": "Apply",
"validityText": null,
"requiredDocumentText": "Required Document",
"isRedirection": null,
"applicationFeeText": "BDT 50,000 for domestic NGOs and USD 9,000 or its equivalent in Bangladeshi Taka for foreign NGOs.",
"slaText": "60 days",
"saveforLaterButtonText": "Save for Later",
"isProvidedByBanglaBiz": false,
"shortName": null,
"serviceType": null,
"fee": "BDT 50,000 for domestic NGOs and USD 9,000 or its equivalent in Bangladeshi Taka for foreign NGOs.",
"redirectApplicationUrl": null,
"requiredDocuments": [],
"processingTime": {
"id": 5858,
"minTime": "50",
"maxTime": "60",
"type": "Days"
},
"metadata": null,

{

"serviceDetailsMenuList": [
{
"id": 1356,
"order": 1,
"tabMenuName": "About",
"description": "The approval process begins with the submission of an application in FD-1 Form to the NGO Affairs Bureau (NGOAB) through online or offline mode. After preliminary scrutiny, the application is forwarded to the Ministry of Home Affairs for security clearance, where investigations are conducted by the National Security Intelligence (NSI) and Special Branch (SB) of Bangladesh Police.\n\nUpon receipt of security clearance, the NGO Affairs Bureau conducts final verification and issues the Registration Certificate. Applications may be submitted through the official portals: ngoab.portal.gov.bd or e-service.ngoab.gov.bd.\n\nFor international NGOs, additional proof of registration in the country of origin is required. During the process, opinions and clearances from relevant ministries or divisions, such as the Finance Division, are obtained where applicable.\n\nAfter approval, NGOs are required to submit project proposals to obtain fund release. Regular audits and periodic reporting are mandatory to ensure transparency, accountability, and compliance with government regulations.",
"title": "About The Approval",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
}
],

{
"id": 432,
"attributes": {
"name": "Repairing Permission of machinery",
"description": "Repairing Permission of Machinery is a service provided by BEPZA that allows companies within EPZs to obtain official approval for repairing machinery and other equipment. Investors must submit an application along with documents specifying the model and serial number of the machinery or equipment to be repaired, as well as the name and address of the workshop performing the repair. BEPZA reviews the submission to ensure compliance with regulatory standards and operational safety guidelines. Upon approval, companies are authorized to carry out repairs legally within the Export Processing Zones, ensuring machinery maintenance, operational efficiency, and adherence to EPZ regulations. This service promotes transparency and proper documentation for all repair activities.",
"shortDescription": "Repairing Permission of machinery\n",
"applicationUrl": "https://www.bepza.gov.bd/",
"isActive": true,
"createdAt": "2025-08-28T10:26:56.148Z",

"serviceDetailsMenuList": [
{
"id": 288,
"order": 1,
"tabMenuName": "About ",
"description": "The Repairing Permission of Machinery is a formal approval process administered by BEPZA for companies operating within Export Processing Zones. To obtain this permission, companies must submit an application seeking approval for repair work along with detailed documentation specifying the model and serial number of the machinery or equipment to be repaired. Additionally, the applicant must provide the name and address of the workshop responsible for carrying out the repair. BEPZA reviews the application to ensure compliance with all regulatory and operational guidelines, verifying that the repair work is safe, documented, and authorized. Upon successful review, the permission is granted, allowing the company to repair its machinery or equipment legally. This approval ensures proper maintenance of industrial machinery, promotes operational efficiency, and maintains regulatory compliance within the EPZs. The typical processing timeline for this service is three working days.",
"title": "About the Approval",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
},

{
"id": 119,
"attributes": {
"name": "Fire license renewal",
"description": "Fire License renewel confirms that the owner has maintain fire safety in his/her business compund according to previous license  issue. ",
"shortDescription": "Fire License renewel confirms that the owner has maintain fire safety in his/her business compund according to previous license  issue. ",
"applicationUrl": "/forms/e-service-bridge/FSCDELN",
"isActive": true,
"createdAt": "2025-08-02T08:58:45.616Z",

"Renewal of Fire License",
"Fire Safety Certificate Renewal",
"Extension of Fire License Validity",

],
"ValidityTime": "1 Year",

"validityText": "1 Year",

},
"serviceDetailsMenuList": [
{
"id": 830,
"order": 1,
"tabMenuName": "About",
"description": "The Fire License Renewal, managed by the Bangladesh Fire Service and Civil Defence (FSCD), is mandatory for all commercial, industrial, or high-rise establishments holding an active Fire Safety Certificate. This renewal confirms that the owner or operator has upheld fire safety standards in the premises, including functional fire prevention equipment, evacuation plans, and compliance with the Fire Prevention and Fire Fighting Act, 2003. It ensures ongoing protection against fire hazards and legal operation of the business.\nThe renewal process is simpler than initial issuance. Applicants must submit key documents: a Treasury Invoice (challan) proving payment of the renewal fee and a copy of the previously approved Fire License. In some cases, an inspection may be conducted to verify continued adherence to safety norms.\nThe standard timeline is approximately 15 working days, though it may vary or extend if departmental manpower is deployed elsewhere, such as in emergencies. Renewal is typically required annually, often before June 30 of each financial year to avoid late fees.\nFees are not fixed and depend on factors like the type of business (e.g., factory, warehouse, or commercial space), building size, height, and risk level assessed by FSCD. Payment is made via government treasury challan.\nTimely renewal is critical for compliance, as it supports other permits and avoids penalties. Applicants should ensure all fire safety installations remain operational to facilitate smooth approval.",
"title": "About The Approval",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
},
{
"id": 831,
"order": 2,
"tabMenuName": "Who can apply",
"description": "Anyone",
"title": "Who can apply",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
}

{
"id": 374,
"attributes": {
"name": "Approval of Drawing & Design of Factory Building",
"description": "Approval of Drawing & Design of Factory Building is an essential authorization process required prior to the construction of an industrial factory building. Through this approval, the proposed architectural and structural drawings are reviewed to ensure compliance with the Bangladesh National Building Code (BNBC), BEPZA Self Constructed Building Requirements, and NFPA fire safety codes. The process aims to ensure structural safety, fire protection, and overall functional suitability of the factory building. All relevant design and drawing documents must be submitted to the Office of the Executive Director (Maintenance), BEPZA, Dhaka for verification and approval. Upon receiving approval within the stipulated timeframe, the construction work can be initiated in accordance with regulatory and safety standards.",
"shortDescription": "Approval of Drawing & Design of Factory Building",
"applicationUrl": "https://www.bepza.gov.bd/",
"isActive": true,
"createdAt": "2025-08-28T09:26:09.453Z",
"updatedAt": "2026-01-25T09:45:59.334Z",
"publishedAt": "2025-08-28T09:26:10.785Z",
"order": 10,
"locale": "en",
"synonyms": [
"Building Plan Approval",
"Factory Design Clearance",
"Architectural Drawing Approval",
"Construction Plan Sanction"
],

},
"serviceDetailsMenuList": [
{
"id": 292,
"order": 1,
"tabMenuName": "About ",
"description": "\nApproval of Drawing & Design of Factory Building is a mandatory regulatory requirement for establishing factory infrastructure within the BEPZA jurisdiction. This approval ensures that the proposed building design meets national and institutional standards related to structural integrity, fire safety, and operational suitability. Applicants are required to prepare and submit detailed architectural, structural, and fire safety drawings in compliance with the Bangladesh National Building Code (BNBC), BEPZA Self Constructed Building Requirements, and NFPA codes.\nThe submitted documents are reviewed by the Office of the Executive Director (Maintenance), BEPZA, Dhaka to verify conformity with safety, engineering, and regulatory guidelines. This approval process helps minimize construction risks and ensures a safe working environment. The standard processing time for this service is 15 days, and no fee is applicable. Once approval is granted, the factory building construction may proceed in a lawful and compliant manner.\n",
"title": "About the Approval",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
},
{

{
"id": 423,
"attributes": {
"name": "Bill Issue, Collection, and Management (From Enterprises)",
"description": "This service involves the issuance, collection, and management of bills from enterprises operating within Bangladesh Economic Zones. It is primarily for utility or service charges, requiring submission of an agreement copy and possession handover certificate (especially for new investors). Bills are issued regularly in the first week of each month, with no applicable fees for the billing process itself. The service ensures transparent financial management through the digital OSS platform.",
"shortDescription": "Bill Issue, Collection, and Management (From Enterprises)\n",
"applicationUrl": "https://www.bepza.gov.bd/",
"isActive": true,
"createdAt": "2025-08-28T10:23:37.006Z",
"updatedAt": "2026-01-25T10:06:39.208Z",

],

]
},
"serviceDetailsMenuList": [
{
"id": 295,
"order": 1,
"tabMenuName": "About ",
"description": "The Bill Issue, Collection, and Management service provided by the Bangladesh Economic Zones Authority (BEZA) handles the systematic billing of enterprises for utilities, land lease, or other services within economic zones. It promotes financial transparency and efficient revenue management, allowing investors to focus on core operations while ensuring timely payments.\nThis process operates under the framework of the Bangladesh Economic Zones Act, 2010 (Act No. 42 of 2010) and relevant One Stop Service rules. For new investors, key documents include a copy of the agreement and the possession handover certificate to initiate accurate billing. Once enterprises are registered and operational, bills are generated and issued automatically in the first week of each month via the digital One Stop Service platform.\nNo separate fees apply for bill issuance or management, as charges reflect actual service consumption. The system supports online viewing, payment tracking, and dispute resolution if needed. Physical consultation is encouraged for clarifications on bill details or adjustments. This recurring monthly process minimizes administrative burdens, facilitates smooth cash flow for zone authorities, and reinforces compliance with investment agreements, contributing to a reliable business environment in Bangladesh's economic zones.",
"title": "About the Approval",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
},
{
"id": 736,
"order": 2,
"tabMenuName": "Who can Apply",
"description": null,
"title": "Who can Apply",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
}
],

"id": 134,

"tabMenuName": "Documents",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
},

"order": 1,
"name": "Agreement Copy. (Possession Hand Over Certificate. (For new Investors)",
"description": null
}
]
}
}
},
{
"id": 487,
"attributes": {
"name": "Approval of the Workers' Welfare Association (WWA) Constitution",
"description": "This service provides approval for the constitution of the Workers' Welfare Association (WWA) within Bangladesh Economic Zones. Applicants must submit an application from the convener of the draft constitution committee addressed to the Executive Chairman, along with three copies of the draft constitution. The process is fully digital via the One Stop Service platform and is completed within 02 working days, ensuring compliance with labor welfare regulations.",
"shortDescription": "Approval of the Workers' Welfare Association (WWA) Constitution\n",
"applicationUrl": "https://www.bepza.gov.bd/",
"isActive": true,
"createdAt": "2025-08-28T10:58:45.101Z",
"updatedAt": "2026-01-25T10:07:12.729Z",

"Worker Association Bylaws",
"Labor Organization Framework"
],

},
"serviceDetailsMenuList": [
{
"id": 296,
"order": 1,
"tabMenuName": "About",
"description": "The Approval of the Workers' Welfare Association (WWA) Constitution service, offered by the Bangladesh Economic Zones Authority (BEZA), enables enterprises in economic zones to establish formal worker welfare bodies as per labor laws. This approval is crucial for promoting employee rights, welfare activities, and harmonious industrial relations within the zones.\nThe process is regulated under the Bangladesh Economic Zones Act, 2010 (Act No. 42 of 2010) and aligned with the One Stop Service framework. Required documents include a formal application by the convener of the draft constitution committee addressed to BEZA's Executive Chairman, accompanied by three copies of the proposed constitution outlining the association's structure, objectives, and operations.\nApplications are submitted digitally through the OSS platform for efficient review. BEZA assesses the draft for compliance with national labor standards, ensuring it supports workers' interests without conflicting with zone regulations. Approval is granted within 02 working days, facilitating quick formation of the WWA.\nPhysical consultation is encouraged for guidance on drafting or clarifications. This service strengthens labor governance, fosters a supportive work environment, and contributes to sustainable industrial growth in Bangladesh's economic zones by empowering workers through organized welfare mechanisms.",
"title": "About the Approval",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
},
{
"id": 741,
"order": 2,
"tabMenuName": "Who can Apply",
"description": null,
"title": "Who can Apply",
"tabMenuIcon": {

}
}
],

}
]

{
"id": 488,
"attributes": {
"name": "WWA Registration & issuance of certificate",
"description": "This service enables the registration of the Workers' Welfare Association (WWA) and issuance of its official certificate within Bangladesh Economic Zones. Applicants must provide details such as the WWA's name and address, formation date, members' information, paid membership application, and three copies of the approved constitution. The fully digital process through the One Stop Service platform ensures completion within 02 working days.",
"shortDescription": "WWA Registration & issuance of certificate\n",
"applicationUrl": "https://www.bepza.gov.bd/",
"isActive": true,
"createdAt": "2025-08-28T10:59:03.203Z",

"serviceDetailsMenuList": [
{
"id": 297,
"order": 1,
"tabMenuName": "About ",
"description": "The WWA Registration and Issuance of Certificate service, provided by the Bangladesh Economic Zones Authority (BEZA), formalizes the establishment of Workers' Welfare Associations in economic zones, promoting organized worker representation and welfare activities in line with national labor laws.\nRegulated under the Bangladesh Economic Zones Act, 2010 (Act No. 42 of 2010) and the One Stop Service framework, this service requires submission of comprehensive details: the association's name and address, date of formation, titles, names, ages, and addresses of members, a complete application listing total paid membership, and three copies of the previously approved constitution.\nApplications are processed digitally via the OSS platform, allowing BEZA to verify compliance with labor standards and zone regulations. Upon satisfactory review, registration is granted, and an official certificate is issued within 02 working days, enabling the WWA to legally operate and undertake welfare initiatives.\nPhysical consultation is encouraged for any guidance needed during preparation. This efficient service supports industrial harmony, protects workers' rights, and contributes to a productive and sustainable work environment in Bangladesh's economic zones by facilitating structured employee welfare mechanisms.",
"title": "About the Approval",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
},

{
"id": 438,
"attributes": {
"name": "Amendment of Permission for taking machine as loan basis (Inter Zone, Intra Zone & DTA)",
"description": "Permission for taking machine as loan basis (Inter Zone, Intra Zone & DTA) is an official approval required for temporarily transferring machinery on a loan basis between EPZ units, zones, or from the Domestic Tariff Area (DTA). This permission ensures that the movement and usage of machinery comply with applicable regulatory and administrative requirements. Applicants must submit an application along with supporting documents such as invoice, agreement executed on non-judicial stamp as prescribed by the Government, and a dues clearance certificate if required. The approval process helps maintain transparency, accountability, and proper record-keeping of machinery movement. Upon obtaining permission within the stipulated timeline, the machinery can be lawfully transferred and utilized for the approved purpose without any applicable fees.",
"shortDescription": "Amendment of Permission for taking machine as loan basis (Inter Zone, Intra Zone & DTA)\n",
"applicationUrl": "https://www.bepza.gov.bd/",
"isActive": true,
"createdAt": "2025-08-28T10:33:42.616Z",
"updatedAt": "2026-01-11T05:03:09.243Z",
"publishedAt": "2025-08-28T10:33:43.969Z",
"order": 3,
"locale": "en",
"synonyms": [
"Machine Loan Amendment",
"Equipment Lending Modification",
"Machinery Transfer Change"
],
"ValidityTime": null,
"applicationButtonText": null,
"validityText": null,
"requiredDocumentText": null,
"isRedirection": null,
"applicationFeeText": null,
"slaText": null,
"saveforLaterButtonText": null,
"isProvidedByBanglaBiz": null,
"shortName": null,
"serviceType": null,
"fee": null,
"redirectApplicationUrl": null,
"requiredDocuments": [],
"processingTime": {
"id": 658,
"minTime": "3",
"maxTime": "5",
"type": "Days"
},
"metadata": null,
"actAndRules": null,
"serviceDetailsMenuList": [
{
"id": 301,
"order": 1,
"tabMenuName": "About the Approval",
"description": "Permission for taking machine as loan basis (Inter Zone, Intra Zone & DTA) is a mandatory administrative approval designed to regulate the temporary transfer and use of machinery among EPZ units, between different zones, or from the Domestic Tariff Area. The objective of this approval is to ensure proper monitoring, control, and compliance with existing rules governing machinery movement and utilization.\nTo obtain this permission, the applicant is required to submit a formal application along with relevant documents, including a valid invoice, a loan agreement executed on non-judicial stamp paper of government-prescribed value, and a dues clearance certificate where applicable. These documents are reviewed by the competent authority to verify ownership, purpose, and compliance status.\nThe standard processing time for this approval is 05 days, and no fee is applicable. Once approved, the machinery may be transferred and used on a loan basis in accordance with the approved terms and conditions.",
"title": "About the Approval",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
}
],

{
"id": 589,
"order": 1,
"actAndRulesType": "Act",
"actAndRulesDetails": "Certificate of Pharmaceutical Product (CPP) / Free Sale Certificate (FSC) is issued by the DGDA to certify that a pharmaceutical product is legally manufactured, marketed, and meets national quality standards. It is mandatory for exporting medicines from Bangladesh. The issuance follows the Drugs Act, 1940, Drugs (Control) Ordinance, 1982, and Drugs Rules, 1945. This certificate ensures compliance with regulatory standards, public safety, and international market acceptance.",

"data": null
}
}

{
"id": 5,
"attributes": {
"name": "Trade License - New",
"description": "For entities located within Economic Zones, the Bangladesh Economic Zones Authority (BEZA) is the competent authority to issue Trade License. Both EZ Unit Investors and EZ Developers shall apply for: Trade License\n\nApplications must be submitted online through the BEZA One Stop Service (OSS) Portal, accompanied by the prescribed documents and applicable fees.\n\nAn application for a Trade License may be submitted after obtaining the Certificate of Incorporation from the Registrar of Joint Stock Companies and Firms (RJSC), or simultaneously while applying for Investment Clearance, and subsequently for other services.\n",
"shortDescription": "Trade License - New",
"applicationUrl": "https://oss.beza.gov.bd/",
"isActive": true,
"createdAt": "2025-07-24T19:49:58.732Z",
"updatedAt": "2026-01-14T11:52:34.709Z",
"publishedAt": "2025-07-24T19:50:00.458Z",
"order": 3,
"locale": "en",
"synonyms": [
"New Business License",
"Commercial License",
"Trading Permit",
"Business Permit"
],
"ValidityTime": "1 year & × A Trade License shall be renewed annually, following the Bangladesh fiscal year (July–June). × Applications for renewal of Trade License must be submitted and completed at least three (03) working days prior to the expiry date of the existing TL/RTL. Can be amended due to changes of authorized persons stated in trade license certificate.",
"applicationButtonText": "Apply ",
"validityText": "1 year & × A Trade License shall be renewed annually, following the Bangladesh fiscal year (July–June). × Applications for renewal of Trade License must be submitted and completed at least three (03) working days prior to the expiry date of the existing TL/RTL. Can be amended due to changes of authorized persons stated in trade license certificate.",
"requiredDocumentText": "Required Documents",
"isRedirection": null,
"applicationFeeText": "BDT 1000-20000 or 5,000-40,000 (depending on the trade activity of the busisiness) Proposed: Based on paid up capital",
"slaText": "7-14 days (approx.)",
"saveforLaterButtonText": "Save for Later",
"isProvidedByBanglaBiz": null,
"shortName": "tl",
"serviceType": "tl",
"fee": "BDT 1000-20000 or 5,000-40,000 (depending on the trade activity of the busisiness) Proposed: Based on paid up capital",
"redirectApplicationUrl": null,
"requiredDocuments": [],
"processingTime": {
"id": 1325,
"minTime": "7",
"maxTime": "14",
"type": "Days"
},

{
"id": 422,
"order": 1,
"tabMenuName": "About",
"description": "The process of issuance and renewal of Trade Licenses shall be carried out in accordance with the following steps:\n  i. The applicant shall apply online through the BEZA-OSS Portal, submitting the duly filled Application Form (FORM-TL-01) along with the prescribed documents and applicable fees.\n  ii. The BEZA One Stop Service Center (OSSC) shall scrutinize, verify, and review the submitted documents and information.\n  iii. If all documents and information are found to be complete and accurate, the Trade License (TL) or Renewal of Trade License (RTL) shall be issued by BEZA OSSC.\n  a. In case of deficiencies or incomplete information, BEZA OSSC shall notify the applicant for submission of additional documents or corrections.\n  b. Upon compliance by the applicant, BEZA OSSC shall issue the TL/RTL Certificate.\n  iv. The applicant shall obtain the digitally signed TL/RTL Certificate by downloading it directly from the BEZA-OSS Portal.",
"title": "About the Approval",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
}
],

{
"id": 1155,
"attributes": {
"name": "Factory license renewal",
"description": "The Renewal Service is provided for the reissuance of a Factory/Establishment/Shop License after the expiry of its validity. This service is carried out by the Department of Inspection for Factories and Establishments under the Ministry of Labour and Employment. The service is generally completed within 45 working days and requires payment of the prescribed renewal fee along with 15% VAT.",
"shortDescription": "The Renewal Service is provided for the reissuance of a Factory/Establishment/Shop License after the expiry of its validity.",
"applicationUrl": "https://dife.gov.bd/",
"isActive": true,
"createdAt": "2026-01-20T11:25:01.720Z",
"updatedAt": "2026-01-20T11:58:05.468Z",
"publishedAt": "2026-01-20T11:25:49.444Z",
"order": 4,
"locale": "en",
"synonyms": [
"Factory License Renewal",
"Industrial License Renewal",
"Renewal of Factory Operation License",
"Manufacturing Establishment License Renewal"
],
"ValidityTime": null,
"applicationButtonText": "Apply",
"validityText": null,
"requiredDocumentText": "Required Document",
"isRedirection": null,
"applicationFeeText": "Pay the license fee (as per Schedule‑7, Bangladesh Labour Rules 2015) via account 1‑3‑143‑0000‑1854, government code, or e-payment, along with 15% VAT.",
"slaText": "45 (Forty-five) working days",
"saveforLaterButtonText": "Save for Later",
"isProvidedByBanglaBiz": false,
"shortName": null,
"serviceType": null,
"fee": "Pay the license fee (as per Schedule‑7, Bangladesh Labour Rules 2015) via account 1‑3‑143‑0000‑1854, government code, or e-payment, along with 15% VAT.",
"redirectApplicationUrl": null,
"requiredDocuments": [],
"processingTime": {
"id": 5192,
"minTime": "40",
"maxTime": "45",
"type": "Days"
},
"metadata": null,
"actAndRules": {
"id": 454,
"title": "Acts and Rules",
"tabMenuName": "Acts and Rules",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
},
"actAndRulesList": [
{

},
"serviceDetailsMenuList": [
{
"id": 938,
"order": 1,
"tabMenuName": "About",
"description": "Renewal of a Factory/Establishment/Shop License is a mandatory process under the Bangladesh Labour Act, 2006 and the Labour Rules, 2015. Since a Factory License is generally valid for one year, failure to renew it before expiry renders the operation of the establishment legally prohibited. The renewal is issued by the Department of Inspection for Factories and Establishments (DIFE) and ensures the safety and welfare of workers.\n\nThe principal documents required for renewal include the lease agreement or updated land record (Hal Khatiyan) (where applicable); National Identity Card or passport of the Owner/Managing Director/Chief Executive Officer (passport in case of foreign nationals); electricity demand note; Memorandum of Articles or Partnership Deed; original treasury challan; original license; list of workers/employees; Fire License; Environmental License/Clearance (where applicable); Boiler License (where applicable); and any other relevant documents.\n\nThe renewal fee must be deposited under the prescribed code as per Schedule‑7 of the Labour Rules or through electronic payment systems (such as Ekpay), along with 15% VAT on the fee. After verification of documents and, where necessary, site inspection, the renewal is generally completed within 45 working days. Failure to renew on time may result in fines or legal complications.",
"title": "About The Approval",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
},
{
"id": 939,
"order": 2,
"tabMenuName": "Who can apply",
"description": "Content Coming Soon",
"title": "Who can apply",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
}
],

{
"id": 472,
"attributes": {
"name": "NOC for foreign/external borrowing",
"description": "NOC for foreign/external borrowing is an official authorization required for enterprises seeking to obtain loans or credit facilities from foreign or external sources. This approval ensures that such borrowing complies with applicable foreign exchange regulations and institutional policies. Applicants must submit a formal application along with an application from the concerned lien bank, supported by all required documents as prescribed under FEID Circular Letter No. 03 dated 06.05.2014 issued by Bangladesh Bank. Additionally, an undertaking executed on non-judicial stamp paper of government-prescribed value is required. The approval process facilitates regulated access to external financing while ensuring transparency and compliance. Upon approval, foreign or external borrowing may proceed within the stipulated timeframe without any applicable fees.",
"shortDescription": "NOC for foreign/external borrowing\n",
"applicationUrl": "https://www.bepza.gov.bd/",
"isActive": true,
"createdAt": "2025-08-28T10:53:19.136Z",
"updatedAt": "2026-01-25T10:18:55.843Z",
"publishedAt": "2025-08-28T10:53:24.569Z",
"order": 30,
"locale": "en",
"synonyms": [
"Foreign Loan NOC",
"External Financing Clearance",
"Offshore Borrowing Permission"
],
"ValidityTime": null,
"applicationButtonText": "Apply",
"validityText": null,
"requiredDocumentText": "Required Documents",
"isRedirection": null,
"applicationFeeText": null,
"slaText": null,
"saveforLaterButtonText": "Save for Later",
"isProvidedByBanglaBiz": null,
"shortName": null,
"serviceType": null,
"fee": null,
"redirectApplicationUrl": null,
"requiredDocuments": [],

"type": "Days"
},
"metadata": null,

}
]
},
"serviceDetailsMenuList": [
{
"id": 305,
"order": 1,
"tabMenuName": "About",
"description": "NOC for foreign/external borrowing is a mandatory regulatory approval designed to oversee and control external financing obtained by enterprises from foreign lenders or international sources. This approval ensures compliance with Bangladesh Bank’s foreign exchange regulations and safeguards the financial and institutional interests of the concerned authorities.\nTo obtain this NOC, the enterprise must submit a formal application along with a corresponding application from the concerned lien bank, including all supporting documents as specified in FEID Circular Letter No. 03 dated 06.05.2014 issued by Bangladesh Bank. An undertaking executed on non-judicial stamp paper of a value prescribed by the Government must also be provided to confirm adherence to the approved terms and regulatory conditions.\nThe submitted documents are reviewed by the competent authority to verify compliance, financial feasibility, and regulatory alignment. The standard processing time for this service is 07 days, and no fee is applicable. Once approved, the enterprise may lawfully proceed with foreign or external borrowing in accordance with the approved conditions.",
"title": "About the Approval",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
},
{
"id": 788,

"tabMenuName": "Who can Apply ",

}

{
"id": 178,
"attributes": {
"name": "Visa Extension Recommendation",
"description": "A unit investor can apply for visa extension recommendation in favor of a foreign national willing to stay more in Bangladesh relating to invest or exploring Business or employment. Upon receiving the visa extension recommendation, the foreign national has to visit the concerned Bangladesh Mission and apply for visa extension. The foreign national (Incumbent) has to present the visa extension recommendation letter and other necessary papers to the concerned office. It is to be noted that a visa extension recommendation from BHTPA does not guarantee extension of visa. Extension of visa is the sole prerogative of the concerned Bangladesh Mission.",
"shortDescription": "Visa Extension Recommendation",
"applicationUrl": "https://ossbhtpa.gov.bd/",
"isActive": true,
"createdAt": "2025-08-04T08:44:45.742Z",
"updatedAt": "2026-01-16T05:32:05.566Z",
"publishedAt": "2025-08-04T08:44:46.550Z",

],

"actAndRulesDetails": "One Stop Service Act, 2018 and One Stop Service (Bangladesh Hi-Tech Park Authority) Regulations, 2019",

}

},
"serviceDetailsMenuList": [
{
"id": 158,
"order": 1,
"tabMenuName": "Who can Apply",
"description": "Applicable for any business entity that has invested in hi-tech activities within any park under the Bangladesh Hi-Tech Park Authority (BHTPA) and intends to operate its business there.\n",
"title": "Who can Apply",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
},
{
"id": 159,
"order": 2,
"tabMenuName": "About",
"description": "Upon receiving a Visa Extension Recommendation request from a unit investor, the Bangladesh Hi-Tech Park Authority (BHTPA) reviews the application and issues a Visa Extension Recommendation Letter in favor of the concerned foreign national. However, BHTPA is not the authority to extend visas. The final approval or rejection of the visa extension is the sole prerogative of the concerned Bangladesh Mission or the Security Services Division (SSD), Ministry of Home Affairs, as per government rules and procedures.\n",
"title": "About the Approval",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
}
],

{
"id": 1122,
"attributes": {
"name": "eNOC Proposed Building",
"description": "The eNOC for Proposed Building is an electronic No Objection Certificate issued by the Bangladesh Fire Service and Civil Defence (FSCD) for planned constructions. It approves the proposed fire safety design and layout before building starts, ensuring compliance with fire prevention standards. Applicants must submit ownership deeds, site plans, highlighted Google maps, fire fighting floor plans (4 sets), engineer certifications, road width proofs, land use clearances, undertakings, and tax receipts. Processing typically takes 60 working days, with fees of 0.30 Taka per square foot for residential and 0.50 Taka for commercial buildings.",
"shortDescription": "eNOC Proposed Building ",
"applicationUrl": "https://fireservice.gov.bd/",
"isActive": true,
"createdAt": "2026-01-19T07:33:21.930Z",
"updatedAt": "2026-01-20T05:40:09.395Z",
"publishedAt": "2026-01-19T07:34:52.918Z",
"order": 7,
"locale": "en",
"synonyms": [
"Proposed Building eNOC",
"eNOC for New Construction",
"Electronic No Objection Certificate for Proposed Building",
"Pre-construction eNOC"
],
"ValidityTime": "Untill the structure is constructed\n",
"applicationButtonText": "Apply",
"validityText": "Untill the structure is constructed\n",
"requiredDocumentText": "Required Document",
"isRedirection": null,
"applicationFeeText": "0.30 tk for every square foot incase of residential building. 0.50 tk for every square foot incase of commercial building. ",
"slaText": "60 working days mostly. But timeline may differ if manpower is engaged elswhere.",
"saveforLaterButtonText": "Save for Later",
"isProvidedByBanglaBiz": false,
"shortName": null,
"serviceType": null,
"fee": "0.30 tk for every square foot incase of residential building. 0.50 tk for every square foot incase of commercial building. ",
"redirectApplicationUrl": null,
"requiredDocuments": [],
"processingTime": {
"id": 5093,
"minTime": "50",
"maxTime": "60",
"type": "Days"
},
"metadata": null,
"actAndRules": {
"id": 408,
"title": "Acts and Rules",
"tabMenuName": "Acts and Rules",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
},
"actAndRulesList": [
{
"id": 411,
"order": 1,
"actAndRulesType": "Act",
"actAndRulesDetails": "BNBC 2020, Fire Prevention Act 2025",
"actAndRulesFile": {
"data": null
}
}
]
},
"serviceDetailsMenuList": [
{
"id": 850,
"order": 1,
"tabMenuName": "About",
"description": "The eNOC (electronic No Objection Certificate) for Proposed Building, provided through the Bangladesh Fire Service and Civil Defence (FSCD) online portal (enoc.fireservice.gov.bd), is a mandatory pre-construction approval for new buildings, particularly multi-storied, commercial, or high-risk structures. It verifies that the proposed design incorporates adequate fire safety measures in line with the Fire Prevention and Extinguishing Act, 2003, and Bangladesh National Building Code (BNBC-2020), allowing safe construction to proceed.\nKey required documents include: ownership deeds (or power of attorney if applicable), layout/site plans, a Google map with the plot highlighted, fire fighting floor plans (submitted in 4 bound sets), membership certificate of the preparing engineer/architect, certification of front road width from city corporation/municipality, land use clearance from relevant authorities, an undertaking on 300 Taka stamp paper to comply with BNBC-2020 and fire laws, firm certification letter, approved plan copies (for existing buildings if relevant), and land development tax payment receipts based on building category.\nThe standard processing timeline is 60 working days, though it may extend if FSCD manpower is engaged in emergencies elsewhere. Fees are area-based: 0.30 Taka per square foot for residential buildings and 0.50 Taka per square foot for commercial ones.\nThis eNOC is the initial step in fire safety compliance, preceding full fire license issuance after construction and installation verification. It promotes proactive risk reduction, facilitates other permits, and ensures buildings are designed for effective fire prevention, detection, and evacuation from the planning stage. Applicants should use enlisted professionals for accurate plan preparation to minimize delays",
"title": "About The Approval",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
},
{
"id": 851,
"order": 2,
"tabMenuName": "Who can apply",
"description": "Anyone",
"title": "Who can apply",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
}
],

"id": 388,
"title": "Document Required",
"tabMenuName": "Documents",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
},

{
"id": 3577,
"order": 2,
"name": "In case of a power of attorney, a copy of the Power of Attorney document.",
"description": null
},
{
"id": 3578,
"order": 1,
"name": "Documents related to ownership of the proposed plot.",
"description": null
},
{
"id": 3567,
"order": 3,
"name": "Layout plan / Site plan.",
"description": null
},
{
"id": 3568,
"order": 4,
"name": "Google map highlighting the proposed plot.",
"description": null
},
{
"id": 3570,
"order": 5,
"name": "Fire fighting floor plan / drawing (4 bound sets).",
"description": null
},
{
"id": 3569,
"order": 6,
"name": "Photocopy of the membership certificate of the engineer/architect who prepared the fire fighting floor plan / drawing.",
"description": null
},
{
"id": 3571,
"order": 7,
"name": "Certification from the City Corporation / Municipality regarding the width of the front road of the proposed plot.",
"description": null
},
{
"id": 3572,
"order": 8,
"name": "Land use clearance from the City Corporation / Municipality / Development Authority (for proposed building).",
"description": null
},
{
"id": 3573,
"order": 9,
"name": "Undertaking on a 300 Taka stamp paper stating that the building will be constructed in compliance with Bangladesh National Building Code (BNBC-2020) and the Fire Prevention and Fire Fighting Act, 2003.",
"description": null
},
{
"id": 3574,
"order": 10,
"name": "Certification letter from the firm must be attached.",
"description": null
},
{
"id": 3575,
"order": 11,
"name": "Photocopy of plans approved by the competent authority for existing buildings.",
"description": null
},
{
"id": 3576,
"order": 12,
"name": "Copy of land development tax receipt paid according to the building category (for proposed building).",
"description": null
}
]
}

{
"id": 25,
"attributes": {
"name": "Visa Recommendation Amendment",
"description": "The Visa Recommendation Amendment service under the Bangladesh Investment Development Authority (BIDA) allows investors, foreign employees, and representatives of BIDA-registered companies or branch/liaison/representative offices to modify or update information previously issued in a visa recommendation letter. This service ensures that any correction, change, or update such as the applicant’s personal details, passport information, designation, company name, duration of stay, or visa category is properly recorded and revalidated by BIDA.\n",
"shortDescription": "Visa Recommendation Amendment",
"applicationUrl": "https://bidaquickserv.org/",
"isActive": true,
"createdAt": "2025-08-02T06:12:59.793Z",
"updatedAt": "2026-01-13T11:14:50.975Z",
"publishedAt": "2025-08-02T06:13:05.040Z",
"order": 3,
"locale": "en",
"synonyms": [
"Visa Modification",
"Visa Endorsement Change",
"Work Visa Amendment"
],
"ValidityTime": "1 Month\n",
"applicationButtonText": "Apply ",
"validityText": "1 Month",
"requiredDocumentText": "Required Documents",
"isRedirection": null,
"applicationFeeText": "Application fee 250 TK and Government fee For industrial 1000 TK + 15% Vat, For Commercial 3000 TK + 15% vat",
"slaText": "3 days",
"saveforLaterButtonText": "Save for Later",
"isProvidedByBanglaBiz": null,
"shortName": "vra",
"serviceType": "VRA",
"fee": "Application fee 250 TK and Government fee For industrial 1000 TK + 15% Vat, For Commercial 3000 TK + 15% vat",
"redirectApplicationUrl": null,
"requiredDocuments": [],
"processingTime": {
"id": 1096,
"minTime": "2",
"maxTime": "3",
"type": "Days"
},
"metadata": null,

},
"actAndRulesList": [
{
"id": 177,

"actAndRulesType": "Act",
"actAndRulesDetails": "Bangladesh Investment Development Authority Act, 2016",
"actAndRulesFile": {
"data": null
}
}
]
},
"serviceDetailsMenuList": [
{
"id": 85,
"order": 2,
"tabMenuName": "Who can Apply",
"description": "The Visa Recommendation Amendment service is intended for foreign investors, expatriate employees, and authorized representatives of companies, branch offices, or liaison/representative offices that are registered with the Bangladesh Investment Development Authority (BIDA). Applicants who have already received a visa recommendation from BIDA but need to correct or update specific information such as name, passport details, designation, company information, visa category, or duration are eligible to apply for an amendment.\n",
"title": "Who can Apply",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
},
{
"id": 86,
"order": 1,
"tabMenuName": "About",
"description": "After submission of the Visa Recommendation Amendment application through the BIDA One Stop Service (OSS) portal, the concerned officer of the Foreign Investment and Registration section reviews all documents and verifies the information provided. If the amendment request is found accurate and justified, BIDA issues an updated Visa Recommendation Letter reflecting the approved changes.",
"title": "About the Approval",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
}
],

"tabMenuName": "Documents",
"tabMenuIcon": {

},

},

},

"description": null
},
{

"description": null
}
]
}

{
"id": 228,
"attributes": {
"name": "Import Permit - Import of Goods / Machinery etc.",
"description": "EZ unit investors must obtain an Import Permit from BEZA whenever they need to bring raw materials, machinery, equipment, or other goods from abroad or from the Domestic Tariff Area into the Economic Zone. This permit is required to ensure legal compliance with BEZA and customs regulations and is a prerequisite for customs clearance through the Bangladesh Single Window (BSW) system.",
"shortDescription": "Import Permit - Import of Goods / Machinery etc.",
"applicationUrl": "https://oss.beza.gov.bd/",
"isActive": true,
"createdAt": "2025-08-04T09:37:01.895Z",

"actAndRulesList": [
{
"id": 185,
"order": 1,
"actAndRulesType": "Act",
"actAndRulesDetails": "1. Bangladesh Economic Zones Act, 2010 (Act No. 42 of 2010); 2. Sub-Rule (2)(a) of Rule 3 of the “One Stop Service (Bangladesh Economic Zones Authority) Rules, 2018” read with Section 11 of the “One Stop Service Act, 2018”",
"actAndRulesFile": {
"data": null
}
}
]
},
"serviceDetailsMenuList": [
{
"id": 427,
"order": 1,
"tabMenuName": "About",
"description": "The objective of this process & approval is to facilitate the smooth and compliant related to import and export of goods for unit investors operating within Bangladesh Economic Zones. Through a structured and transparent procedure, BEZA ensures that only duly registered and qualified companies are granted the Import Permit required for customs clearance. Brief Synopsis of the process of approval: \n\nSTEP-1: Application Submission\nApplicant submits application online through Bangladesh Single Window (BSW) at bswnbr.gov.bd along with required documents.\n\nSTEP-2: Automated Scrutiny\nBSW Risk Management System (RMS) performs automated checks and Application and documents are forwarded to BEZA One Stop Service (OSS) Portal.\n\nSTEP-3: Decision and Issuance\nBEZA-OSS reviews documents and information. If in order → Import Permit issued. If discrepancies (HS code mismatch, quantity, missing docs) → Shortfall Notice sent for correction.\n\nSTEP-4: Forward to Customs\nUpon approval, BEZA-OSS portal automatically forwards the permit to the BSW system for customs clearance processing.\n\nPre-Conditions for Applicants: \n-The applicant company must obtain Investment Clearance and a Trade License from the BEZA-OSS portal.\n-The company must obtain a Business Identification Number (BIN) and complete registration in the BSW system prior to applying for an Import Permit and simultaneously update BIN information in BEZA-OSS Portal. \n-The company is required to provide periodic updates on investment and employment data through the BEZA-OSS portal. Failure to provide such data will result in ineligibility to apply for both Import Permits and Export Permits.\n-For importing construction materials, Finish schedule and specific quantity of the materials required to be assessed and subsequently approved by the Building Construction and Occupancy Committee, BEZA.",
"title": "About the approval",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
},
{
"id": 428,
"order": 2,
"tabMenuName": "Who can Apply",
"description": "Pre-Conditions for Applicants: \n  - The applicant company must obtain Investment Clearance and a Trade License from the BEZA-OSS portal.\n  - The company must obtain a Business Identification Number (BIN) and complete registration in the BSW system prior to applying for an Import Permit and simultaneously update BIN information in BEZA-OSS Portal. \n  - The company is required to provide periodic updates on investment and employment data through the BEZA-OSS portal. Failure to provide such data will result in ineligibility to apply for both Import Permits and Export Permits.\n  - For importing construction materials, Finish schedule and specific quantity of the materials required to be assessed and subsequently approved by the Building Construction and Occupancy Committee, BEZA.\n  Remarks:\n  - EZ Unit Investor and EZ Developer do not required IRC/ERC License from Office of the Chief Controller of Imports and Exports (CCIE).\n  - EZ Unit Investor and EZ Developer are highly encouraged to consult with BEZA-OSSC before importing capital machinery, construction materials etc. \n  - Any misinformation and invalid documents in the application may be terminated.\n  - The working days shall be counted from the day of the complete and final submission of the SOP [complete information and attached documents].\n  - A shortfall message shall be sent to the applicant in case of an incomplete submission. If the applicant does not respond in due course, the submitted application shall be discarded.\n  - BEZA-OSS reserves the right to request additional documents or clarifications where necessary.",
"title": "Who can Apply",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
}
],

"description": "Drug License (Retail/Wholesale Medicine Sale License) – Issued by the Directorate General of Drug Administration (DGDA), this license provides legal permission to stock, sell, and distribute medicines in pharmacies or drug stores. It ensures the quality of medicines and safeguards public health.",

"serviceDetailsMenuList": [
{
"id": 954,
"order": 1,
"tabMenuName": "About",
"description": "Drug License is issued by the Directorate General of Drug Administration (DGDA) and is mandatory for the retail or wholesale sale, storage, and distribution of Allopathic, Unani, Ayurvedic, and Homeopathic medicines. Conducting a pharmaceutical business without this license is illegal. This license is crucial for ensuring drug quality control, preventing misuse, and safeguarding consumer health.\n\nRequired Documents:\nApplication completed in the prescribed Form-7\nCertificate and undertaking of a Registered Pharmacist\nTrade license\nShop rental agreement or ownership document\nBank solvency certificate\nApplicant’s national identity card\nOther relevant documents, such as TIN certificate and experience certificates\nApplications can be submitted online via the ADLRS portal.\nProcessing Time: Generally 60 working days.\nRenewal Fees:\nAllopathic medicines: BDT 2,500 in municipal/metropolitan areas; BDT 1,500 in union-level areas\nUnani/Ayurvedic/Homeopathic medicines: BDT 2,000 and BDT 1,000 respectively\nFees must be paid through Treasury Chalan.\nThis license plays a vital role in ensuring the orderly operation of the pharmaceutical business.",
"title": "About the Approval",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
},
{
"id": 955,
"order": 2,

"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
}

"description": "Security Clearance in BIDA refers to a mandatory verification process conducted by the Security Services Division (SSD) under the Ministry of Home Affairs of Bangladesh. It is required for foreign nationals who wish to work, invest, or stay in Bangladesh. {
"id": 1070,
"attributes": {
"name": "Foreign Exchange Compliance",
"description": "Sonali Bank's Foreign Exchange Compliance service ensures all foreign exchange transactions adhere to Bangladesh Bank's Guidelines for Foreign Exchange Transactions (GFET) and circulars. It encompasses inward/outward remittances, foreign currency account management (FC, RFCD, NFCD, ERQ), export/import settlements, travel/medical/education quotas, and endorsements.\nThe bank operates dedicated authorized dealer branches and a wide network for compliant processing, including reporting to regulators. With subsidiaries like Sonali Exchange (USA) and partnerships worldwide, it supports NRB remittances, wage earners' funds, and corporate forex needs. This service prevents misuse, facilitates legitimate flows, and promotes foreign exchange earnings through secure channels, instant crediting options, and regulatory approvals where required, serving individuals, exporters, importers, and expatriates efficiently.",
"shortDescription": "Foreign Exchange Compliance",
"applicationUrl": "https://sonalibank.com.bd/",
"isActive": true,
"createdAt": "2026-01-16T09:58:40.353Z",
"updatedAt": "2026-01-16T11:05:25.078Z",
"publishedAt": "2026-01-16T10:03:39.979Z",
"order": 2,
"locale": "en",
"synonyms": [
"Foreign Exchange Regulatory Compliance",
"Exchange Control Compliance",
"Foreign Currency Compliance"
],
"ValidityTime": "Inward: Instant to same-day; Outward/standard: 1-5 working days; BB prior approval cases: Longer (up to weeks).",
"applicationButtonText": "Apply",
"validityText": "Inward: Instant to same-day; Outward/standard: 1-5 working days; BB prior approval cases: Longer (up to weeks).",

{
"id": 20,
"attributes": {
"name": "Project Office New",
"description": "A New Permission Form for the Project Office (Bida Commercial Wing) is a short administrative document used to request and authorize new activities, access, or changes within the Bida Commercial Wing. It captures essential details about the requester, the nature and purpose of the request (e.g., access, construction, fit-out, vendor work, events), location and duration, any safety or compliance requirements, and required approvals. The Project Office reviews the form to verify completeness, assess risks and compliance, obtain necessary signatures from stakeholders (security, facilities, safety, commercial management), and issue a formal permission or rejection. The form helps ensure coordinated, safe, and compliant operations in the commercial wing.\n",
"shortDescription": "Project Office New",
"applicationUrl": "https://bidaquickserv.org/",
"isActive": true,
"createdAt": "2025-07-29T07:30:31.789Z",
"updatedAt": "2026-01-13T12:28:46.176Z",

],

"slaText": "16 days",
"saveforLaterButtonText": "Save for Later",
"isProvidedByBanglaBiz": null,
