"order": 1,
"name": "Application Online by the Incumbent through OSS Portal in prescribed Form;",
"description": null
},
{
"id": 1821,
"order": 2,
"name": "Copy of Passport of the Incumbent with at least 6 months validity (if not furnished earlier);",
"description": null
},
{
"id": 1823,
"order": 3,
"name": " Photo of the Incumbent, if not furnished earlier, (JPG/JPEG/PNG; 300X300 Pixel Less than 500KB);",
"description": null
},
{
"id": 1824,
"order": 4,
"name": "Signature of the Incumbent, if not furnished earlier (JPG/JPEG/PNG; 80X300 Pixels, Less than 500KB);",
"description": null
},
{
"id": 1822,
"order": 5,
"name": " Copy of Memorandum of Articles (in case of PI Visa) or Expression of Interest Letter for visiting BHTPA by the interested foreign national, if not furnished earlier;",
"description": null
}
]

{
"id": 1151,
"attributes": {
"name": "Factory Layout Plan Approval",
"description": "Factory Layout Plan Approval is a service for approving the design of the internal layout of an industrial factory building, including machinery, production lines, and worker movement. This service is provided by the Department of Inspection for Factories and Establishments (DIFE). The service is free of charge and is usually completed within 45 working days.",
"shortDescription": "Factory Layout Plan Approval is a regulatory service provided by the Department of Environment (DoE)",
"applicationUrl": "https://dife.gov.bd/",
"isActive": true,
"createdAt": "2026-01-20T09:24:19.215Z",

"serviceDetailsMenuList": [
{
"id": 928,
"order": 1,
"tabMenuName": "About",
"description": "Factory Layout Plan approval is a vital service for the establishment or operation of an industrial facility in Bangladesh, provided by the DIFE. Through this approval, the internal layout of the factory—including machinery placement, production processes, safe movement pathways for workers, fire safety arrangements, and related aspects—is reviewed to ensure safety and compliance with required standards.\n\nThe required documents for application include, as applicable, a copy of the Trade License; lease agreement or updated land record (Hal Khatiyan); National ID card or passport of the owner/Managing Director/Chief Executive Officer (passport in case of foreign nationals); soil test report; structural design and drawings prepared by a licensed engineer; load-bearing capacity certificate; building construction certificate; and an approved building plan from the local authority.\n\nThis service is provided completely free of charge. Following document verification and, where necessary, site inspection, the Factory Layout Plan approval is generally issued within 45 working days. Operating a factory without this approval may pose legal risks and can create obstacles in obtaining other licenses, such as Fire License and Environmental Clearance Certificate.",
"title": "About The Approval",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
},
{

"order": 2,
"tabMenuName": "Who can apply",
"description": "Content Coming Soon\n",
"title": "Who can apply",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
}

{
"id": 133,
"attributes": {
"name": "Approval for Partial Use of Building",
"description": "The actual allotted land may differ from the requested area. After receiving the Land Allotment Letter, the investor must submit a Land Use and Utility Connection Plan for clearance and, within 3 months of signing the Land Lease Agreement, submit the Master Plan, Design, Drawings, and Soil Test Report for approval. Once design approval is granted, construction may begin, and investors may apply through the BHTPA OSS Portal for permission to partially use the building during construction if needed.",
"shortDescription": "Approval for Partial Use of Building",

{
"id": 445,
"order": 1,
"tabMenuName": "About the Approval ",
"description": "The approval process ensures that all land development and construction activities comply with BHTPA’s standards and guidelines.\n After receiving the Land Allotment Letter, investors must obtain clearance for their Land Use and Utility Plans, followed by approval of the Master Plan, Designs, and Soil Test Report.\n Once approved, construction can begin, and investors may seek BHTPA’s permission through the OSS Portal for partial use of the building if required.",

{
"id": 203,
"attributes": {
"name": "Project Registration Renewal",
"description": "To invest in BHTPA, obtaining a Project Registration is must. The Project Registration is the process of accepting the proposed project as an investment under Bangladesh Hi-Tech Park Authority (BHTPA). A Project Registration Certificate is issued against each of the Unit Investor upon completion of Project Registration Process through the BHTPA OSS Portal. The Project Registration Certificate will be initially valid for 3 (Three) years subject to the fulfillment of the terms and conditions. The Project Registration shall be renewed for every 2 (Two) years after the expiry of the certificate. For renewal, the company shall have to apply for Project Registration Renewal 3 (three) months prior to the date of expiry of the certificate. Land and space allotted in favor of this project will be automatically cancelled upon expiry of project registration. This Project Registration ID and any other ID generated in reference to this Project Registration ID will be considered invalid and unusable from the date of Expiry of the Project Registration. All Recommendations, Certificates, Registrations, Permits, NOCs, Licenses and Letters issued by BHTPA in favor of the project will be considered null and void after the date of expiry of the Project Registration.\n",
"shortDescription": "Project Registration Renewal",
"applicationUrl": "https://ossbhtpa.gov.bd/",
"isActive": true,
"createdAt": "2025-08-04T09:07:17.235Z",
"updatedAt": "2026-01-25T08:17:45.126Z",
"publishedAt": "2025-08-04T09:07:18.572Z",
"order": 48,
"locale": "en",
"synonyms": [
"Project Revalidation",
"Renewal of Registration"
],

{
"id": 378,
"attributes": {
"name": "Recommendation for Permanent Bond License",
"description": "The Recommendation for Permanent Bond License is a service provided by BEPZA to enable investors to operate bonded warehouse facilities within Export Processing Zones (EPZs) on a permanent basis. This license allows investors to store imported machinery, raw materials, and accessories under customs supervision. Investors must submit an application along with supporting documents, including the temporary bond registration, lists of imported machinery, and raw materials with H.S. codes. BEPZA reviews these submissions to ensure compliance with regulatory and customs requirements. Upon approval, a recommendation for the permanent bond license is issued. The process is designed to be efficient, typically completed within 2 days, allowing investors to manage their industrial inputs seamlessly and facilitate uninterrupted production activities.",
"shortDescription": "Recommendation for Permanent Bond License",
"applicationUrl": "https://www.bepza.gov.bd/",

"serviceDetailsMenuList": [
{
"id": 281,
"order": 1,
"tabMenuName": "About ",
"description": "The Recommendation for Permanent Bond License is a formal approval granted by BEPZA that authorizes investors to establish and operate permanent bonded warehouse facilities within an EPZ. These facilities allow the storage of imported machinery, raw materials, and accessories under customs control, ensuring regulatory compliance and smooth project operations. To obtain this recommendation, investors are required to submit a completed application along with supporting documents, including a copy of the temporary bond registration, detailed lists of imported machinery, and raw materials with H.S. codes for proper classification. BEPZA evaluates these submissions to confirm adherence to EPZ and customs regulations before issuing the recommendation. Once approved, the investor can apply for the Permanent Bond License from the relevant customs authority. This service is crucial for ensuring uninterrupted industrial operations, proper management of imported goods, and maintaining compliance with regulatory standards. The typical processing time for this service is 2 working days, providing a swift and transparent procedure for investors.",
"title": "About the Approval",

}

{
"id": 184,
"attributes": {
"name": "Local Sales Permit",
"description": "All Hi-Tech Parks will be considered and treated as Warehousing Station as per the Bangladesh Hi-Tech Park Warehousing Station Rules-2015. Business/Industrial Enterprises may enjoy the Bonded warehouse facilities in the following manner. The Products produced in the Hi-Tech Parks using the raw materials imported from outside or collected from inside the country under bonded facilities, can be sold for home consumption subject to the following: \n a) shall have to pay applicable taxes and duties on the relevant raw materials;\n b) Shall have to pay applicable VAT on the supply of products, in case of sale or transfer in the local market. \n Provided that, 100% export oriented industrial establishment are not allowed to sell their excess products as specified by the National Board of Revenue, for home consumption. (S.R.O. No. 58-Law//2018/83/Customs, Date: 12 February 2018). \n Industrial Unit of Hi-Tech Parks can enjoy the Bonded facilities and can import Capital Machineries and Spare Parts under Bonded facilities of Bangladesh Hi-Tech Park Warehousing Station Rules-2015. Once an Industrial Unit is declared as Bonded Industry, it is allowed to handed over the Capital Machineries and Spare Parts to any other bonded industry and to any non-bonded Industry under certain terms and conditions as stated in the Office Order No. 023/2021/Customs/, Nothi No.-08.01.0000.56.01.001.2020/238(4), Dated 22-09-2021 of NBR. \n In such cases the Business/Industrial Enterprises shall apply to the Authority in a prescribed Form (as attached at the end of the document) through BHTPA OSS Portal for seeking permission for the Local Sales Permit.",
"shortDescription": "Local Sales Permit",
"applicationUrl": "https://ossbhtpa.gov.bd/",
"isActive": true,
"createdAt": "2025-08-04T08:48:51.646Z",
"updatedAt": "2026-01-25T08:03:29.119Z",
"publishedAt": "2025-08-04T08:48:52.453Z",
"order": 25,
"locale": "en",
"synonyms": [
"Domestic Purchase Authorization",
"Local Procurement Permit"
],

}

{
"id": 167,
"order": 1,
"tabMenuName": "About the Approval",
"description": "An Industrial Unit in a Hi-Tech Park can apply for bonded facilities and Local Sales Permit through the BHTPA OSS Portal.\n \n BHTPA reviews the application to ensure compliance with the Bangladesh Hi-Tech Park Warehousing Station Rules-2015 and related Customs/NBR regulations.\n \n Once satisfied, BHTPA issues approval, allowing the unit to import capital machinery, spare parts, or sell products locally under bonded conditions, while ensuring taxes, duties, and VAT are properly applied.\n",
"title": "About the Approval",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
}

{
"id": 370,
"attributes": {
"name": "Allotment of Industrial Plot/ SFB/ Piece of Land",
"description": "The Allotment of Industrial Plot, Single Factory Building (SFB), or a piece of land is a service provided by BEPZA to facilitate investors in establishing their industrial projects within Export Processing Zones (EPZs). Investors are required to submit an application along with a sketch of the desired location. Upon submission, the application is reviewed, and the plot or SFB is allotted based on availability and suitability. This process ensures orderly development within the EPZ and provides investors with the legal authorization to occupy the allocated industrial space. The entire process is designed to be transparent and efficient, typically completed within 5 working days, allowing investors to commence project activities promptly.",
"shortDescription": "Allotment of Industrial Plot/ SFB/ Piece of Land",
"applicationUrl": "https://www.bepza.gov.bd/",

"serviceDetailsMenuList": [
{
"id": 277,
"order": 1,
"tabMenuName": "About ",
"description": "The allotment of an industrial plot, Single Factory Building (SFB), or a piece of land within an EPZ is a formal approval granted by BEPZA to investors for establishing their industrial facilities. Investors must submit a completed application along with a sketch of the proposed location, detailing the desired plot or building. BEPZA reviews the application to ensure compliance with zoning, availability, and suitability criteria. Once the evaluation is complete, the industrial space is formally allotted to the investor, granting them legal rights to occupy and utilize the allocated plot or SFB. This approval is crucial for investors to initiate project implementation, construct factory buildings, and operate within the EPZ framework. The service is designed to facilitate orderly development, maintain regulatory compliance, and promote investor confidence. The process is typically completed within 5 working days, ensuring timely commencement of industrial activities.",
"title": "About the Approval",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
},

{
"id": 405,
"attributes": {
"name": "Cancellation of Import Permit",
"description": "Cancellation of Import Permit is a service provided by BEPZA to allow investors or companies to formally revoke an import permit when it is no longer required or valid. Applicants must submit a written application explaining the reasons for cancellation along with the original set of the import permit. BEPZA reviews the submission to ensure that the cancellation request is legitimate and complies with regulatory requirements. Upon approval, the import permit is officially cancelled, preventing further use of the permit for importing goods. This service ensures proper documentation, regulatory compliance, and transparency in import management, providing investors with an efficient mechanism to manage their trade permits within Export Processing Zones (EPZs).",
"shortDescription": "Cancellation of Import Permit\n",
"applicationUrl": "https://www.bepza.gov.bd/",
"isActive": true,
"createdAt": "2025-08-28T10:09:34.787Z",

"serviceDetailsMenuList": [
{
"id": 283,
"order": 1,
"tabMenuName": "About ",
"description": "The Cancellation of Import Permit is a formal process administered by BEPZA to revoke an import permit issued to investors or companies within EPZs. Applicants are required to submit a complete application specifying the valid reasons for cancellation along with the original import permit set. BEPZA reviews the application to verify that the cancellation request meets regulatory guidelines and ensures no pending obligations are linked to the permit. Upon verification, the authority officially cancels the import permit, which prevents its further use for importing goods. This process safeguards compliance with trade regulations, maintains accurate permit records, and supports efficient management of import activities. It also protects both the investor and the regulatory authority by ensuring that outdated or unused permits are properly revoked. Typically, the approval process is completed within three working days, providing a timely and transparent service for EPZ-based businesses.",
"title": "About the Approval",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
},

{
"id": 1052,
"attributes": {
"name": "Drug Manufacturing License",
"description": "Factory Layout Plan Approval is a regulatory service provided by the Department of Environment (DoE). It evaluates the environmental suitability, safety measures, and pollution control systems of a factory’s layout plan and issues clearance, ensuring sustainable production.",
"shortDescription": "Factory Layout Plan Approval is a regulatory service provided by the Department of Environment (DoE)",
"applicationUrl": "https://dgdagov.info/",
"isActive": true,
"createdAt": "2026-01-02T09:35:54.643Z",
"updatedAt": "2026-01-23T20:17:22.577Z",
"publishedAt": "2026-01-02T09:35:55.971Z",
"order": 4,
"locale": "en",
"synonyms": [
"Pharmaceutical Manufacturing License",
"Drug Production Permit",
"Medicine Manufacturing Authorization",
"Pharmaceutical Operation License"
],
"ValidityTime": null,
"applicationButtonText": "Apply ",
"validityText": null,
"requiredDocumentText": "Required Documents",
"isRedirection": null,
"applicationFeeText": "New License Fee: Biological-1,00,000/-(One Lakh Taka) Non-Biological-50,000/-(Fifty Thousand Taka)",
"slaText": "Processing Time: 120 working days from the receipt of complete application.",
"saveforLaterButtonText": "Save for Later",
"isProvidedByBanglaBiz": false,
"shortName": "dml",
"serviceType": "dml",
"fee": "New License Fee: Biological-1,00,000/-(One Lakh Taka) Non-Biological-50,000/-(Fifty Thousand Taka)",
"redirectApplicationUrl": null,
"requiredDocuments": [],
"processingTime": {
"id": 6008,
"minTime": "100",
"maxTime": "120",
"type": "Days"
},
"metadata": null,
"actAndRules": {
"id": 469,
"title": "Acts And Rules",
"tabMenuName": "Acts And Rules",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
},
"actAndRulesList": [
{
"id": 473,
"order": 1,
"actAndRulesType": "Act",
"actAndRulesDetails": "The Drug Manufacturing License in Bangladesh is regulated under the Drugs Act, 1940, the Drugs (Control) Ordinance, 1982, and the Drugs Rules, 1945, which govern the manufacture, quality control, and distribution of pharmaceutical products. All manufacturers must comply with Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) Guidelines and ensure that all drugs are registered under the Registration of Drugs Rules, 2006 before marketing. The Directorate General of Drug Administration (DGDA) is the licensing authority responsible for issuing, monitoring, and enforcing these regulations. Operating without a valid license is legally prohibited and subject to penalties or prosecution.",
"actAndRulesFile": {
"data": null
}
}
]
},
"serviceDetailsMenuList": [
{
"id": 970,
"order": 1,
"tabMenuName": "About The Approval ",
"description": "Drug Manufacturing License is issued by the Directorate General of Drug Administration (DGDA) and is mandatory for the production of biological and non-biological drugs under the Drugs Act, 1940 and the Bengal Drugs Rules. No entity can commence drug manufacturing without this license, and compliance with Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) is required.\n\nThe required documents for application include the prescribed forms (Form-16 for biological drugs, Form-13 for non-biological drugs; application Forms-12/15), the company’s trade license, land ownership deed or lease agreement, factory design/layout plan, list of equipment, employee details (certificates of pharmacists/chemists), fire service and environmental clearance, bank solvency certificate, tax/TIN certificate, police clearance, and a description of the drug manufacturing process (Annexure). A detailed list of documents is available on the DGDA website (dgda.gov.bd) and the ADLRS portal.\n\nUpon receipt of a complete application, the processing time is 120 working days, which includes site inspection. The license fees for new applications are: BDT 100,000 for biological drugs and BDT 50,000 for non-biological drugs (inclusive of 15% VAT); payment is deposited to the bank under code 1271500001863 and the original treasury challan must be submitted.",
"title": "About The Approval ",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
},
{
"id": 975,
"order": 2,
"tabMenuName": "Who can apply",
"description": "Content coming soon ",
"title": "Who can apply",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
}
],

{
"id": 373,
"attributes": {
"name": "Physical Possession Handing Over of Plot/ SFB",
"description": "Physical Possession Handing Over of Plot/SFB is an official process through which the allotted plot or Standard Factory Building (SFB) is formally handed over to the investor after completion of all contractual and financial requirements. This service confirms the legal transfer of physical possession, enabling the investor to commence development or operational activities. Applicants must submit required documents, including treasury chalan, proof of security deposit payment, BEPZA registration fee documents (if applicable), and authorization letter where necessary. The handover is conducted at the BEPZA Complex, Dhanmondi, Dhaka, Bangladesh. Upon fulfillment of all conditions, physical possession is handed over within the stipulated timeline through proper documentation and authorization.",
"shortDescription": "Physical Possession Handing Over of Plot/ SFB",
"applicationUrl": "https://www.bepza.gov.bd/",
"isActive": true,
"createdAt": "2025-08-28T09:25:26.939Z",
"updatedAt": "2026-01-25T09:44:30.469Z",
"publishedAt": "2025-08-28T09:25:28.319Z",
"order": 7,
"locale": "en",

"Site Possession Transfer",
"Physical Site Handover"
],

"serviceDetailsMenuList": [
{
"id": 291,
"order": 1,
"tabMenuName": "About ",
"description": "Physical Possession Handing Over of Plot/SFB is a mandatory administrative approval that finalizes the allotment process of industrial land or Standard Factory Building under BEPZA. This approval ensures that all financial, legal, and procedural obligations related to the lease agreement have been duly fulfilled before granting physical possession to the investor.\nTo obtain this approval, the applicant must submit the original copy of the treasury chalan as stamp duty (in case of land lease), valid documents confirming payment of the security deposit, BEPZA registration fee documents for new industries, and an authorization letter if the lease agreement is signed by a representative other than the shareholder. All activities related to this service are carried out at the BEPZA Complex, Dhanmondi, Dhaka.\nThe standard processing time for this service is 02 working days. Payments are made through bank using the prescribed chalan code and designated BEPZA account. Once approved, physical possession of the plot or SFB is formally handed over, allowing the investor to proceed with project implementation lawfully and without delay.",
"title": "About the Approval",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
},
{

{
"id": 40,
"attributes": {
"name": "General Export registration certificate",
"description": "Export Registration Certificate (ERC) is a mandatory authorization required for exporting goods from Bangladesh for commercial purposes. It is issued by the Chief Controller of Imports & Exports (CCI&E). Applications can be submitted through the Online Licensing Module (OLM) portal at olm.ccie.gov.bd, and the service is generally processed within three (3) working days.",
"shortDescription": "Export Registration Certificate (ERC) is a mandatory authorization required for exporting goods from Bangladesh for commercial purposes.",
"applicationUrl": "https://olm.ccie.gov.bd/",
"isActive": true,
"createdAt": "2025-08-02T06:31:43.533Z",
"updatedAt": "2026-01-25T11:11:16.984Z",
"publishedAt": "2025-08-02T06:31:45.449Z",

"Authorized Export Registration",

],
"ValidityTime": null,

"applicationFeeText": "The fee prescribed for the service described in Appendix–C can be paid online through an e-Challan via the OLM system after submitting the application.",

"serviceDetailsMenuList": [
{
"id": 872,
"order": 1,
"tabMenuName": "About",
"description": "Export Registration Certificate (ERC) is a mandatory authorization required for any individual or business entity to export goods from Bangladesh for commercial purposes. It is issued under the authority of the Chief Controller of Imports & Exports (CCI&E) through the Online Licensing Module (OLM) portal.\n\nThe application process is entirely online. Applicants must complete the prescribed application form on the OLM portal and upload color scanned copies of the required documents. Key documents include a treasury challan covering the registration fee and 15% VAT, a valid trade license, e-TIN certificate, bank solvency certificate from a nominated bank, chamber of commerce or relevant trade association membership certificate, National Identity Card, any additional licenses required for specialized businesses, partnership deed (for partnerships) or Certificate of Incorporation and Form XII (for limited companies), and the latest income tax return acknowledgment receipt.\n\nThe prescribed fee must be paid online through the portal in the form of an e-challan. Upon successful verification of documents, the ERC is generally issued within three (3) working days. Commercial export activities are not legally permitted without a valid ERC, and the certificate is subject to annual renewal.",
"title": "About The Approval",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
},
{
"id": 873,
"order": 2,
"tabMenuName": "Who can apply",
"description": " Content Coming Soon",
"title": "Who can apply",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
}
],

"attributes": {
"name": "Attestation and Certification of Import Documents for Investor Company",
"description": "Once having Investment Project Registration at BHTPA OSS Portal, an Investor Company can apply for Attestation of Import Documents and Certification against import of Goods/Materials from BHTPA for availing the incentive of exemption of import duties, regulatory duties, supplementary duties and value added tax according to the S.R.O. No. 212-Law//2015/49/Customs, Date: 01 July, 2015 of the government, each time prior to release of the imported Goods/Materials from the Customs authority of the port of entry.",
"shortDescription": "Attestation and Certification of Import Documents for Investor Company",

"id": 502,

"tabMenuName": "About The Approval ",
"description": "After obtaining Investment Project Registration through the BHTPA OSS Portal, an Investor Company can apply for Attestation of Import Documents and Certification from BHTPA.\n \n This approval confirms that the imported goods or materials are eligible for exemption from import duties, regulatory duties, supplementary duties, and VAT under S.R.O. No. 212-Law//2015/49/Customs, Date: 01 July 2015.\n \n The attestation must be obtained each time before the release of imported goods from the customs authority at the port of entry.",

"data": null
}
},

{
"id": 1083,
"attributes": {
"name": "Project Registration Cancellation by Authority",
"description": "To invest in BHTPA, obtaining a Project Registration is must. The Project Registration is the process of accepting the proposed project as an investment under Bangladesh Hi-Tech Park Authority (BHTPA). Project Registration at BHTPA does not guarantee Land or Space allotment at any Hi-Tech or IT Park. The BHTPA reserves the right to reject any proposed project without showing any reason. The Project Registration Application fee is Non-Refundable. A Project Registration Certificate declares that BHTPA considers the proposed project as a genuine investment. The Project Registration Certificate will have to be used as the main reference as an investor at BHTPA. Allotted land and/or space may not match with the proposed/expected land and/or space in the Project Registration application. \n However, after having a project registered at BHTPA and even having allotted of land and/or Space in any Park, BHTPA can cancel the Project Registration at any time if BHTPA is convinced to do the same without showing any reason. BHTPA can do this when the terms and conditions as set forth in the Project registration Certificate and/or in the lease agreement (if any) are not fulfilled properly by the Investor and/or as decided by the Authority. Once the Project Registration is cancelled, all the activities of the project will be cancelled too. All land and space allotted in favor of this project will be automatically cancelled upon expiry or cancellation of project registration.",
"shortDescription": "Project Registration Cancellation by Authority",
"applicationUrl": "https://ossbhtpa.gov.bd/",
"isActive": true,
"createdAt": "2026-01-17T04:44:32.901Z",
"updatedAt": "2026-01-25T08:20:21.839Z",
"publishedAt": "2026-01-25T08:20:21.831Z",
"order": 51,
"locale": "en",
"synonyms": [
"Authority-Initiated Project Cancellation",
"Project Registration Revocation",
"Official Project Registration Cancellation",
"Regulatory Cancellation of Project Registration"
],
"ValidityTime": "N/A",

"validityText": "N/A",

{
"id": 163,
"attributes": {
"name": "NOC for Substation Establishment",
"description": "Once having Investment Project Registration at BHTPA OSS Portal, a Park Developer or a unit investor can apply for having a No objection Certificate (NoC) from BHTPA for establishing a Substation, which is deemed necessary for them. Before establishing a Substation the Park Developer or unit investor needs to consider, whether it is feasible or justified in terms of technical and physical point of view. \n \n However, an Investor can apply for No objection Certificate (NoC) for establishing a Substation through the BHTPA OSS Portal once it is registered in the Portal and have been allocated a piece of Land.",
"shortDescription": "NOC for Substation Establishment",
"applicationUrl": "https://ossbhtpa.gov.bd/",
"isActive": true,
"createdAt": "2025-08-04T08:32:13.596Z",
"updatedAt": "2026-01-25T08:10:35.203Z",
"publishedAt": "2025-08-04T08:32:14.529Z",
"order": 33,
"locale": "en",
"synonyms": [

"Electrical Setup Clearance"
],
"ValidityTime": "Valid till the validity of Project Registration",

{
"id": 126,
"order": 1,
"tabMenuName": "About",
"description": "The approval of a No Objection Certificate (NoC) for establishing a Substation ensures that the proposed installation is technically feasible and aligns with BHTPA’s safety and infrastructure standards.\n After obtaining Project Registration and Land Allotment, the Park Developer or investor can apply for the NoC through the BHTPA OSS Portal.\n BHTPA reviews the technical justification and site suitability before granting approval to proceed with substation installation.\n",
"title": "About the Approval",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
}
],

{
"id": 208,
"attributes": {
"name": "Space Allotment Cancellation by Authority",
"description": "Once having Investment Project Registration at BHTPA OSS Portal, a unit investor can apply for Space Allotment in accordance to the proposed plan within 15 (Fifteen) days of issuance of the Project Registration Certificate. However, actual allotted Space may not match with the area requested in the Project Registration application. However, after issuing a Space Allotment Certificate, BHTPA can cancel the Space Allotment Certificate at any time if BHTPA is convinced to do the same without showing any reason or can cancel the Space Allotment Certificate as requested by the Allottee. Settlement of the issue may be resolved as per the terms and conditions as stated in the space rent agreement/lease agreement (if any).",
"shortDescription": "Space Allotment Cancellation by Authority",
"applicationUrl": "https://ossbhtpa.gov.bd/",
"isActive": true,
"createdAt": "2025-08-04T09:10:57.756Z",

{
"id": 209,
"attributes": {
"name": "Work Permit Cancellation by Authority",
"description": "Once a work permit is issued by the authority against a foreign national, the authority can cancel the work permit as and when required under the following circumstances: \n a) If any work permit holder is found to be engaged in any anti-state or law and order or any other anti-social/immoral activities;\n b) If the work permit holder misbehaves with the workers and/or engages in any activity against the interests of the Organization/Park and/or if his work performance is not satisfactory;\n c) If any objection is raised by Bangladesh Bank against any work permit holder due to violation of any section of Foreign Exchange Control Act, the Authority can cancel the work permit of the foreign nationals in that case as well;\n d) If there is any objection regarding the security clearance of a work permit holder, the authority can cancel his work permit;\n e) If any false information is provided in the application form for work permit, the Authority can also cancel the work permit.",
"shortDescription": "Work Permit Cancellation by Authority",
"applicationUrl": "https://ossbhtpa.gov.bd/",
"isActive": true,
"createdAt": "2025-08-04T09:11:48.031Z",

{
"id": 259,
"attributes": {
"name": "Commercial Operation",
"description": "When an Economic Zone (EZ) Unit Investor intends to commence commercial operations, the investor shall be required to submit an application for the issuance of a Certificate of Commencement of Commercial Operation (CCCO) through the BEZA One Stop Service (OSS) Portal in online.\n  Prior to submission of the application, the investor must:\n  \n  - Successfully complete its trial operation/production phase, thereby demonstrating readiness for full-scale commercial operation; and\n  - Obtain all mandatory regulatory approvals, licenses, and services from the respective service-providing authorities/agencies, as prescribed under the applicable laws, rules, and policies.\n  \n  Only upon fulfillment of the above conditions shall the application for the CCCO be considered admissible for processing by BEZA. The date specified in the CCCO shall constitute the official date of commencement of commercial operations, and shall be duly recognized by BEZA and all other relevant authorities for legal, regulatory, and fiscal purposes.",
"shortDescription": "When an Economic Zone (EZ) Unit Investor intends to commence commercial operations,",
"applicationUrl": "https://oss.beza.gov.bd/",
"isActive": true,
"createdAt": "2025-08-04T10:11:42.640Z",
"updatedAt": "2026-01-24T05:54:10.742Z",
"publishedAt": "2025-08-04T10:11:43.361Z",
"order": 18,
"locale": "en",
"synonyms": [
"Business Operation",
"Commercial Activity",
"Operational Commencement",
"Business Commencement"
],
"ValidityTime": "Once",
"applicationButtonText": "Apply",
"validityText": "Once",
"requiredDocumentText": "Documents required",
"isRedirection": null,
"applicationFeeText": "× Application Fee: BDT 500 (Five Hundred Taka only).   × 15% Value Added Tax (VAT) on application fee.   × Applicable bank charges shall apply.   × iv. An additional 15% VAT shall be levied on bank charges",
"slaText": "7 working days",
"saveforLaterButtonText": "Save for Later",
"isProvidedByBanglaBiz": null,
"shortName": null,
"serviceType": "coc",
"fee": "× Application Fee: BDT 500 (Five Hundred Taka only).   × 15% Value Added Tax (VAT) on application fee.   × Applicable bank charges shall apply.   × iv. An additional 15% VAT shall be levied on bank charges",
"redirectApplicationUrl": null,
"requiredDocuments": [],
"processingTime": {
"id": 3360,
"minTime": "5",
"maxTime": "7",
"type": "Days"
},
"metadata": null,

},
"actAndRulesList": [
{
"id": 278,
"order": 1,
"actAndRulesType": "Act",
"actAndRulesDetails": "Sub-Rule (2)(a) of Rule 3 of the “One Stop Service (Bangladesh Economic Zones Authority) Rules, 2018” read with Section 11 of the “One Stop Service Act, 2018”,",
"actAndRulesFile": {

},
"serviceDetailsMenuList": [
{
"id": 616,
"order": 2,
"tabMenuName": "Who can Apply",
"description": "Economic Zone (EZ) Unit Investors \n\n-        Successfully complete its trial operation/production phase, thereby demonstrating readiness for full-scale commercial operation; and\n-        Obtain all mandatory regulatory approvals, licenses, and services from the respective service-providing authorities/agencies, as prescribed under the applicable laws, rules, and policies.\n\nOnly upon fulfillment of the above conditions shall the application for the CCCO be considered admissible for processing by BEZA. The date specified in the CCCO shall constitute the official date of commencement of commercial operations, and shall be duly recognized by BEZA and all other relevant authorities for legal, regulatory, and fiscal purposes.",
"title": "Who can Apply",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
},
{
"id": 617,
"order": 1,
"tabMenuName": "About",
"description": "Step 1: Application Submission\n  • The Applicant submits the application online through the BEZA-OSS Portal.\n  • All required documents and the prescribed fees must be uploaded/paid at the time of submission.\n  Step 2: Application Review by BEZA-OSS\n  • BEZA-OSS reviews the application for completeness.\n  If the application is complete, BEZA-OSS schedules an inspection date and notifies the Applicant.\n  If the application is incomplete, BEZA-OSS notifies the Applicant to submit the missing or corrected documents.\n  Step 3: On-Site Inspection\n  • On the scheduled date, the BEZA Inspection Committee visits the factory/establishment.\n  • The Committee verifies:\n  - Trial production status,\n  - Valid approvals and licenses,\n  - Building and structural compliance,\n  - Machinery installation,\n  - Fire safety and environmental standards.\n  Step 4: Inspection Report & Recommendation\n  • The Inspection Committee prepares a written inspection report.\n  • The report, with recommendations (approve, approve with conditions, or reject/defer), is submitted to the Executive Chairman of BEZA.\n  Step 5 – Decision & Issuance of CCCO\n  • Based on the inspection report, the Competent Authority issues a decision.\n  • If approved, BEZA-OSS generates and issues the Certificate of Commencement of Commercial Operation (CCCO) through the OSS Portal.\n  Step 6 – Download of CCCO\n  • The Applicant logs into the BEZA-OSS Portal.\n  • The digitally signed CCCO is downloaded and retained by the Applicant for official use.",
"title": "About The Approval",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
}
],

{
"id": 1422,
"attributes": {
"name": "Foreign exchange compliance",
"description": "Prime Bank's Foreign Exchange Compliance service ensures all inward and outward foreign exchange transactions, including remittances, comply with Bangladesh Bank's Guidelines for Foreign Exchange Transactions (GFET) and related circulars. It covers inward remittances (primarily NRB/wage earners via global partners for instant credit or cash payout), outward remittances (commercial payments, travel, education, medical, etc.), foreign currency endorsements, and FC account management. The bank maintains a dedicated NRB operation unit for efficient processing, with wide distribution through branches, agents, and digital channels. Compliance involves verification of purpose, supporting documents, and reporting to regulators. This service supports seamless, secure, and regulatory-compliant international fund transfers for individuals, exporters, importers, and corporates, promoting financial inclusion and foreign exchange earnings.",
"shortDescription": "Prime Bank's Foreign Exchange Compliance service ensures all inward and outward foreign exchange transactions.",
"applicationUrl": null,
"isActive": true,

"tabMenuName": "About",
"description": "The approval process under Prime Bank's Foreign Exchange Compliance service refers to the regulatory verification and authorization required for foreign exchange transactions, particularly outward remittances and certain inward disposals, in line with Bangladesh Bank guidelines.\nApprovals are handled by authorized branches or central units to ensure adherence to GFET-2018 and circulars, preventing misuse while facilitating legitimate flows.",

{
"id": 1418,
"attributes": {
"name": "Remittance Approval",
"description": "The Remittance Approval service enables customers to receive inward foreign remittances efficiently at BRAC Bank. Required documents include purpose-wise supporting documents (if applicable), Form C (if applicable), and invoice/agreement (if applicable). Documents can be submitted at any Branch or via email to enquiry@bracbank.com. For BRAC Bank account holders, processing occurs on the same day if complete and compliant documents are received before 2:00 PM. For customers of other banks, documents must be received before 1:00 PM. Charges apply as per the bank's Schedule of Charges, with payment credited via Account Credit, EFT, or RTGS.",
"shortDescription": "The Remittance Approval service enables customers to receive inward foreign remittances efficiently at BRAC Bank.",
"applicationUrl": null,
"isActive": true,

"serviceDetailsMenuList": [
{
"id": 1467,
"order": 1,
"tabMenuName": "About",
"description": "The Remittance Approval service at BRAC Bank facilitates the swift and compliant receipt of inward foreign remittances from abroad. It caters to both individual and corporate customers, ensuring funds are credited securely and promptly in accordance with Bangladesh Bank regulations. Key required documents vary based on the remittance purpose but typically include supporting documents specific to the transaction (if applicable), Form C for certain categories (if applicable), and relevant invoice or agreement (if applicable). This ensures full regulatory compliance and smooth processing. Customers can conveniently submit documents at any BRAC Bank Branch or digitally via email to enquiry@bracbank.com, offering flexibility and ease of access. The processing timeline is designed for efficiency: BRAC Bank account holders receive same-day crediting if complete and compliant documents are submitted before 2:00 PM. For customers holding accounts with other banks, documents must be received and verified before 1:00 PM to enable same-day processing. Applicable fees are levied as per BRAC Bank's prevailing Schedule of Charges. Funds are credited through secure modes such as direct Account Credit, Electronic Fund Transfer (EFT), or Real Time Gross Settlement (RTGS), ensuring quick availability.\nThis service provides a reliable, transparent, and customer-friendly channel for receiving international remittances, supporting personal transfers, export proceeds, salaries, and other legitimate inward flows.",
"title": "About the approval",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
},
{
"id": 1468,
"order": 2,
"tabMenuName": "Who can apply",
"description": null,
"title": "Who can apply",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}

{
"id": 1424,
"attributes": {
"name": "Remittance Approval",
"description": "The Remittance Approval service enables lawful outward remittances from Bangladesh in strict adherence to the Guidelines for Foreign Exchange Transactions (GFET-2018) issued by Bangladesh Bank and related circulars. It supports a wide range of remittance purposes, including payment for imports, technical services, royalties, dividends, consultancy fees, and other regulatory-approved overseas transactions.\nRequired documents vary depending on the specific nature of the remittance but typically include a duly filled Overseas Remittance Application Form, TM Form, relevant appendices as outlined in GFET-2018, commercial invoice, agreement/contract, and applicable regulatory approvals (e.g., from BIDA, BTRC, or NGO Affairs Bureau). Additional supporting documents such as trade license, certificate of incorporation, memorandum and articles of association, tax/VAT challans, job completion certificates, or prior/post permissions from Bangladesh Bank/BIDA may also be required on a case-by-case basis.\nDocuments can be submitted at any Branch, through the assigned Relationship Manager (RM), or at the Corporate Marketing Office (CMO). The approval timeline is highly efficient, with processing completed within 0-1 working day upon receipt of complete and compliant documentation.\nCharges comprise a SWIFT fee of BDT 500.00 plus 15% VAT, stationery charge of BDT 500.00 plus 15% VAT, correspondent bank charges as per their schedule, and a TT commission capped at BDT 500.00 in line with Bangladesh Bank circulars. Additional taxes and VAT are applied based on the remittance purpose, following the latest NBR Circular and Finance Act 2023. This service ensures transparent, compliant, and swift execution of international payments for corporate and individual clients.",
"shortDescription": "The Remittance Approval service enables lawful outward remittances from Bangladesh in strict adherence.",
"applicationUrl": null,
"isActive": true,
"createdAt": "2026-01-26T13:35:44.501Z",
"updatedAt": "2026-01-26T15:51:37.761Z",
"publishedAt": "2026-01-26T13:37:29.809Z",
"order": 0,
"locale": "en",
"synonyms": null,
"ValidityTime": null,
"applicationButtonText": "Apply",
"validityText": null,
"requiredDocumentText": "Required Documents",
"isRedirection": null,
"applicationFeeText": "SWIFT: BDT 500 + 15% VAT; Stationery: BDT 500 + 15% VAT; Correspondent Bank Charges: As per their schedule; TT Commission: Max BDT 500 (per BB circular); Tax & VAT: As per OTT purpose (per NBR Circular & Act 2023)",
"slaText": "0-1day",
"saveforLaterButtonText": "Save for Later",
"isProvidedByBanglaBiz": false,
"shortName": null,
"serviceType": null,
"fee": "SWIFT: BDT 500 + 15% VAT; Stationery: BDT 500 + 15% VAT; Correspondent Bank Charges: As per their schedule; TT Commission: Max BDT 500 (per BB circular); Tax & VAT: As per OTT purpose (per NBR Circular & Act 2023)",
"redirectApplicationUrl": null,
"requiredDocuments": [],
"processingTime": null,

"serviceDetailsMenuList": [
{
"id": 1487,
"order": 1,
"tabMenuName": "About",
"description": "The Remittance Approval service enables lawful outward remittances from Bangladesh in strict adherence to the Guidelines for Foreign Exchange Transactions (GFET-2018) issued by Bangladesh Bank and related circulars. It supports a wide range of remittance purposes, including payment for imports, technical services, royalties, dividends, consultancy fees, and other regulatory-approved overseas transactions. Required documents vary depending on the specific nature of the remittance but typically include a duly filled Overseas Remittance Application Form, TM Form, relevant appendices as outlined in GFET-2018, commercial invoice, agreement/contract, and applicable regulatory approvals (e.g., from BIDA, BTRC, or NGO Affairs Bureau). Additional supporting documents such as trade license, certificate of incorporation, memorandum and articles of association, tax/VAT challans, job completion certificates, or prior/post permissions from Bangladesh Bank/BIDA may also be required on a case-by-case basis. Documents can be submitted at any Branch, through the assigned Relationship Manager (RM), or at the Corporate Marketing Office (CMO). The approval timeline is highly efficient, with processing completed within 0-1 working day upon receipt of complete and compliant documentation. Charges comprise a SWIFT fee of BDT 500.00 plus 15% VAT, stationery charge of BDT 500.00 plus 15% VAT, correspondent bank charges as per their schedule, and a TT commission capped at BDT 500.00 in line with Bangladesh Bank circulars. Additional taxes and VAT are applied based on the remittance purpose, following the latest NBR Circular and Finance Act 2023. This service ensures transparent, compliant, and swift execution of international payments for corporate and individual clients.",
"title": "About the approval",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
},
{
"id": 1488,
"order": 2,
"tabMenuName": "Who can apply",
"description": null,

}
}

{
"id": 273,
"attributes": {
"name": "Security clearance related services - Security Clearance for Work Permit",
"description": "All foreign nationals/expatriates employed in companies under the jurisdiction of the Bangladesh Economic Zones Authority (BEZA) must obtain Security Clearance from the Ministry of Home Affairs (MoHA), after securing a valid Work Permit.\n Security Clearance is a mandatory requirement for visa extension, immigration compliance, and lawful continuation of employment in Bangladesh. Applications must be submitted through the BEZA One Stop Service (OSS) Portal within twenty-one (21) days of obtaining the Work Permit. Failure to do so may result in complications regarding visa extension and other immigration matters. Otherwise, it may arise complications regarding visa extension and any other immigration related issues in future.",
"shortDescription": "Security clearance related services - Security Clearance for Work Permit",
"applicationUrl": "/investor/security-clearance",
"isActive": true,
"createdAt": "2025-08-04T10:20:01.495Z",
"updatedAt": "2026-03-04T04:30:21.043Z",
"publishedAt": "2025-08-04T10:20:02.229Z",
"order": 25,
"locale": "en",
"synonyms": [
"Work Permit Security Check",
"Employment Security Clearance",
"Foreign Worker Background Check"
],
"ValidityTime": "New : One Year\nExtension: 4 Year Max",
"applicationButtonText": "Apply",
"validityText": "New : One Year\nExtension: 4 Year Max",
"requiredDocumentText": "Required Documents",
"isRedirection": null,
"applicationFeeText": "BDT 500 + 15% VAT  - Applicable bank charges + 15% VAT on bank charges",
"slaText": "\"Preparation of Security Report:  × 30 days – by NSI  × 45 days – by SB  Issuance of Security Clearance (NOC):  × 3 working days – MoHA, upon receipt of both reports.\"",
"saveforLaterButtonText": "Save for Later",
"isProvidedByBanglaBiz": null,
"shortName": "BZSCREQ",
"serviceType": "BZSCREQ",
"fee": "BDT 500 + 15% VAT  - Applicable bank charges + 15% VAT on bank charges",
"redirectApplicationUrl": null,
"requiredDocuments": [],
"processingTime": {
"id": 3788,
"minTime": null,
"maxTime": null,
"type": "Days"
},
"metadata": null,

"serviceDetailsMenuList": [
{
"id": 243,
"order": 1,
"tabMenuName": "About",
"description": "1. The employer/company, on behalf of the expatriate, submits the Security Clearance application to Ministry of Home Affairs through the BEZA-OSS Portal, along with all required documents.\n 2. The MoHA reviews the submitted information and documents, and assigns the application to the National Security Intelligence (NSI) and the Special Branch (SB) for preparation of the Security Report.\n 3. Upon receipt of the Security Reports from NSI and SB, MoHA processes and issues the Security Clearance Certificate (NOC) in favor of the expatriate.\n 4. The applicant (employer/company) obtains the Security Clearance Certificate both:\n × Via registered email; and\n × By downloading it directly from the BEZA-OSS Portal",
"title": "About the Approval",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
},
{
"id": 244,
"order": 2,
"tabMenuName": "Who can Apply",
"description": "All expatriates intending to work in companies established within the jurisdiction of the Bangladesh Economic Zones Authority (BEZA) must obtain a valid Work Permit through the BEZA-OSS Portal. The Work Permit is issued based on visa type and is mandatory for lawful employment in Economic Zones.",
"title": "Who can apply",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
}
],

{
"id": 94,
"attributes": {
"name": "Exporter registration",
"description": "Exporter Registration Service is provided by the Export Promotion Bureau (EPB), through which individuals and organizations are issued a registration certificate for the purpose of exporting goods from Bangladesh. Applications must be submitted online via www.ems.epb.gov.bd\nRequired documents include:\nTwo (2) passport-sized photographs of the applicant,\nImport Registration Certificate (IRC) or ERC (if available), and\nMembership certificate from the relevant Chamber/Association.\nOnce a complete application is submitted, processing generally takes 7 working days. The applicable fee of BDT 2,000 must be paid online or deposited into the designated EPB Administrative Fund account at Janata Bank, Sonali Bank, or BRAC Bank.",
"shortDescription": "Exporter Registration Service is provided by the Export Promotion Bureau (EPB)",

"serviceDetailsMenuList": [
{
"id": 1022,
"order": 1,
"tabMenuName": "About",
"description": "Through the Exporter Registration Service of the Export Promotion Bureau (EPB), Bangladeshi entrepreneurs and organizations can register as official exporters and avail various benefits. Exporting without this registration is not permitted, and without it, exporters cannot access services such as the Certificate of Origin (CO), GSP facilities, cash incentives, participation in international trade fairs, and other government incentives.\nThe approval process is entirely online. For new registration, applications must be submitted through the Export Management System (EMS) portal: www.ems.epb.gov.bd\n. Applicants must submit:\nTwo (2) recent passport-sized photographs,\nValid IRC/ERC (if applicable), and\nCurrent membership certificate of the relevant Chamber of Commerce or Association.\nInspection forms, if required, can be downloaded from www.epb.gov.bd\nOnce the application and fee are submitted, EPB verifies compliance with export policies. Generally, approval and issuance of the registration certificate are completed within 7 working days.\nThe registration fee is BDT 2,000, payable online or deposited into the designated EPB bank accounts:\nJanata Bank: 0100005936980\nSonali Bank: 0117202001003\nBRAC Bank: 1503202417031001\nThis registration enhances credibility in international markets and plays a vital role in boosting Bangladesh’s export growth.",
"title": "About The Approval",
"tabMenuIcon": {
"data": null
}
},

"order": 2,

"order": 1,
"id": 1,
"tabMenuName": "About",
"description": "The Bangladesh Investment Development Authority (BIDA) acts as the central investment promotion and facilitation agency under the Prime Minister’s Office.\n BIDA Registration serves as the primary business approval for establishing an industrial unit or service enterprise in Bangladesh. The registration confirms that the business meets national investment guidelines and allows the investor to access various government services such as import permits, utilities, land acquisition, and other statutory clearances.\nIt is a prerequisite for both local entrepreneurs and foreign investors intending to start or expand a business in Bangladesh under the country’s investment laws and policies.",
"title": "About the approval",

"description": "The following entities are eligible to apply for BIDA Registration: Local entrepreneurs establishing a new industrial or service-oriented project. "validityText": "The BIDA Registration remains valid for the lifetime of the project, subject to compliance with applicable investment laws and any periodic reporting requirements. In case of project expansion or major changes, the investor must update the registration information with BIDA.",
